date: Reviewed in the United States on April 10, 2026
title: The $9 Audiophile Secret: Small Dongle, Big Practicality
body: Let’s be honest: nobody actually wants to live the "dongle life." When the headphone jack started disappearing from our devices, most of us were annoyed at the thought of yet another accessory to lose. But after spending a significant amount of time with the Apple USB-C to 3.5 mm Headphone Jack Adapter, I’ve realized it’s less of an annoyance and more of a practical hero in my daily toolkit. Whether I’m grading student dissertations or jumping between devices for lectures, this little white cable has become surprisingly indispensable. The "Hidden" Audio Quality As someone who appreciates high-fidelity gear, I was skeptical. I’ve used high-end headphones from brands like Beyerdynamic and Audio-Technica, and usually, you’d expect a $9 adapter to muddy the sound. Consumer feedback often points this out, but the reality is a pleasant surprise: this isn't just a physical adapter; it’s a tiny, highly efficient Digital-to-Analog Converter (DAC). In the audiophile community, this dongle is actually legendary for its transparency. It has an incredibly low output impedance, which means if you’re like me and enjoy using sensitive in-ear monitors (IEMs), you won’t hear that annoying background hiss that plagues cheaper adapters. The sound is neutral and clean—it doesn't try to "color" the music, which is exactly what I want when I’m focusing on work. Read more Read more

product_name: Apple EarPods Headphones with USB-C Plug, Wired Ear Buds with Built-in Remote to Control Music, Phone Calls, and Volume
date: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2026
title: Fit issues
body: If you’ve used an iPhone from before the USB-C switch, you already know these EarPods. They’re plug-and-play and easy to use. The sound is decent, with solid bass and clear treble for a basic wired pair. The main issues are fit, durability, and price. The fit isn’t great for everyone. The angle doesn’t always sit right, so they can sound a bit thin unless you adjust them. I usually need earbud covers to keep them in place, and even then I sometimes have to press them slightly forward or back to get the best sound. Durability is hit or miss. After about a year, the rubber near the Y-junction where the wires split tends to loosen. Once that happens, the wiring feels exposed and more fragile. I’ve had a few pairs fail this way, and it’s usually cheaper to replace them than repair them. The price also feels high for such an old design. They’re okay and mostly reliable, but the fit issues, weak point at the Y-junction, and unchanged pricing are real drawbacks.

date: Reviewed in the United States on March 23, 2026
title: My Thoughts As a Samsung "Fanboy"
body: Like the title says, I'm a Samsung guy through and through. But, out of curiosity, I ordered these EarPods just to try them out. I gotta say, these are really good earbuds, especially for the price. They fit perfectly in my ears, and works with most USB C devices. I use them with my Galaxy S24 Ultra, and my older Tab S7 without any issues. My brother recently got an iPhone, and said they work perfectly well (of course) for him. Sound quality is pretty good regardless of what you listen to. While they're not made for audiophiles, I found these to be above average relative to other earbuds in the same price range. The built-in mic works fine for phone calls and video chatting, and never had anyone complain about any sort of issue on their end. I like that it has buttons for volume, media control, etc. Noise cancellation isn't the best, but it's to expected due to how they fit in your ears. All in all, whether you're on Team Apple or Team Android, I highly recommend these EarPods!

title: Clásicos, confiables y actualizados
body: Los Apple EarPods con conector USB-C representan una actualización significativa de los tradicionales EarPods, adaptándose a los dispositivos más recientes de Apple que han optado por este estándar. Ofrecen una opción económica para quienes buscan una experiencia auditiva básica pero confiable. El diseño de los EarPods sigue la geometría del oído, lo que proporciona una comodidad superior en comparación con los auriculares tradicionales. Su forma ergonómica permite un ajuste adecuado sin necesidad de almohadillas adicionales, siendo ideales para uso prolongado sin causar molestias., incluso para hacer ejercicio sin tanto problema en que sufran una caída. Estos EarPods admiten audio sin pérdidas, con una resolución de hasta 24 bit/48 kHz, lo que significa que pueden reproducir música en alta calidad, similar a la de un estudio de grabación. Aunque no ofrecen una experiencia auditiva de gama alta, proporcionan un sonido claro y nítido adecuado para la mayoría de los usuarios. Son compatibles con dispositivos Apple que cuentan con puerto USB-C, incluyendo modelos recientes de iPhone, iPad y Mac. Además, incorporan un control remoto integrado que permite ajustar el volumen, controlar la reproducción de música y responder llamadas con facilidad, lo cual es bastante cómodo y práctico en pleno 2025. date: Reviewed in the United States on February 20, 2026
title: Nice upgrade to a very useful device
body: I love Apple devices and smart home appliances. As such, I was a very early adopter of the original AirTags and have used them while traveling (tucked inside of luggage) and in my daily life for years. The second generation of the AirTag represents a small, but worthwhile change to the function of the device. The ability to precisely track an object is noticeably better and the speaker inside pings very so slightly louder (I personally don’t buy the 50% louder claim, but there is a difference). If you’re the type to have keys slip behind couch cushions or get buried under laundry, this is the change you’ve been waiting for. The 2nd generation AirTag keeps the exact same footprint (both weight and size) so it fits perfectly into any existing accessories or holders you might have. Set up is a breeze and the Find My app works like a charm. I was able to see my checked bag move throughout a major airport and its arrival at my destination using the app. You can also disable forgotten item notifications easily if you happen to be leaving luggage in a hotel and don’t want to be pinged if you head out for the day. This AirTag weighs next to nothing and is worth slipping into a bag or attaching to a keychain for peace of mind. It seems just as durable as the first generation which I have toted all over the planet. Read more Read more

title: Simple, Reliable Peace of Mind
body: Setup was effortless. My phone detected the tag automatically and walked me through the whole process. If that doesn't happen for some reason, you can add it manually through the Find My app, but either way it takes about a minute. Just keep in mind you need an iPhone for any of this to work. I use mine on my dog's collar as a safety net in case he ever gets out and gets lost. Thankfully I haven't had to use it in an emergency yet, but I've tested it and it works exactly as advertised. There's real peace of mind in knowing it's there. One thing worth mentioning that doesn't get talked about enough: the battery needs to be changed periodically. It's a simple swap and the tag will notify you when it's getting low, but if you ignore those alerts and the battery dies, the tag is useless when you need it most. Easy to manage, just don't set it and forget it completely. Overall a great product. Simple to use and does exactly what it promises.

body: The Apple AirPods 4 are really impressive overall, and I’d give them a solid four stars. Music sounds full, voices are clear, and they perform well for both calls and everyday listening. As expected, the connectivity is seamless. They pair instantly with Apple devices, switch easily between them, and just work without any hassle. That level of convenience is a big plus and one of the main reasons to go with AirPods. However, the biggest downside for me is the fit. They just didn’t sit quite right in my ears. After wearing them for a while, they started to hurt, especially during longer listening sessions. I noticed pressure and discomfort that made it hard to keep them in for extended periods. I also didn’t feel like they were as secure as I’d like, which makes me hesitant to use them during workouts or when moving around a lot. Overall, these earbuds sound great and have all the convenience you’d expect from Apple, but the fit just wasn’t right for me. If they were more comfortable, they’d easily be a five-star product. title: All day comfort and reliable sound that feels effortless
body: I bought these because I wanted a pair of wireless earbuds that could handle long listening sessions, workouts, and everyday calls without constant fiddling. The redesign with a shorter stem and a softer contour looked like it would sit more stably in my ears, and the quick press controls seemed handy for skipping tracks or taking a call on the go. Pairing in day to day use was straightforward. I just opened the charging case near my phone and tapped connect, and they were ready to go. They stay in place during workouts and commutes, and the ear sensor pauses audio when I take them out which is convenient for quick interruptions or when I need to listen to something short without fumbling with the device. What stood out most is the overall clarity of calls and the way music and shows feel more immersive with spatial audio that moves with my head. The sound isn't harsh at higher volumes and the voice isolation helps keep conversations clear even in busy environments. Battery life is solid too with about five hours per charge and a total of around 30 hours with the case, and the charging case itself charges via USB-C which is convenient during travel. A small caveat is the learning curve with the quick press controls, which took a moment to get used to. The case is noticeably smaller than the previous generation, but it still adds a bit of bulk when tucked into a pocket. Overall, they feel reliable, comfortable, and genuinely enjoyable to use, delivering a five star experience. Read more Read more

She was always on the move and making tons of noise that was ear piercing for me. When she unloaded the dishwasher it was a special kind of hell in my ears. She shopped for ear buds that would save my sanity, and she hit the jackpot with these. We spoke the other day and I was in shock - I couldn't hear her dog barking, her dishes clanking or....the final convincing moment: I could not hear her blowdryer! (I asked her to turn it on as a test). I had to buy them! I read reviews that said the fit was better so I figured I would give them a try. Last thing: This version has features I never knew I needed but LOVE. What we liked: • Noise cancellation is impressive: She says it blocks out almost all background noise, which is perfect for studying in dorms, libraries, or coffee shops • Sound quality: Clear, balanced audio for both music and calls • Comfortable fit: She can wear them for long periods without discomfort • Easy pairing: Seamless connection with her iPhone and other Apple devices • Battery life: Lasts through classes and study sessions without constantly needing to recharge Things to consider: • A little pricey, but you definitely get what you pay for Read more Read more

product_name: Apple AirPods Pro 3 Wireless Earbuds, Active Noise Cancellation, Live Translation, Heart Rate Sensing, Hearing Aid Feature, Bluetooth Headphones, Spatial Audio, High-Fidelity Sound, USB-C Charging
date: Reviewed in the United States on April 1, 2026
title: A Solid Product At An Incredible Price
body: I've had two sets of professional hearing aids in the past, one of them being fairly new, but these are my new favorite even though they are far less expensive. I will warn that pairing to Android devices can be somewhat difficult (seems to work best if you pair before pairing to your Apple device), but I was able to pair to both my Samsung S25+ and Samsung Watch 8 Classic (the 25+ being the more difficult). Particularly nice is "Adaptive" mode, which changes the setting automatically when it hears real conversation (not just TV conversation), but oddly you have to change the setup so that you can switch to that mode by just touching the device. That is a real game changer, because it eliminates the stray white-type noise you hear with hearing aids, and you just hear your surroundings in a passthrough mode. Battery life seems to exceed the 10 hours claimed using them in Adaptive mode with the hearing aid function on. The hearing aid testing was fairly easy, but I question its accuracy since it showed too similar of results for my left and right ear, when there is a difference at high frequencies. Doesn't seem to affect the overall operation though. My main complaint which almost results in a one star reduction is the lousy user documentation. Rather than just having a webpage that covers this item it's combined with all the other similar devices. And it really doesn't explain that well what the various settings do. Finally, it doesn't seem to be available in just a PDF version that you can download. Another item to note is switching between Android devices is manual, although that may be an advantage. product_name: Apple AirPods Pro 3 Wireless Earbuds, Active Noise Cancellation, Live Translation, Heart Rate Sensing, Hearing Aid Feature, Bluetooth Headphones, Spatial Audio, High-Fidelity Sound, USB-C Charging
date: Reviewed in the United States on November 11, 2025
title: A subtle but significant upgrade for iOS users.**UPDATE**
body: I am an ear bud junky and have been on a quest for the perfect set for a long time. I have owned and own a lot of the big names from Apple, B&0, B&W, Sony, Bose, Klipsch, Devialet, Technics, many off brands, and more. I use both iOS and Android devices and don’t have a particular favorite so I can speak to how ear buds interact with both. This is an interesting update. I almost passed on it and I probably should have as my AirPod Pro 2’s are just fine. However, they don’t fit me perfectly and there has been a lot made of the new fit these have. My oldest son will be getting the 2’s. These look and feel a lot like the 2’s. The physical design is nearly identical. However, the upgrades, while subtle, are mostly notable. I will break these down below and try to help those that are deciding on whether the upgrade is worth your money. Sound: Those that have read any of my other reviews on buds, know I always start with sound. It is the primary function of an ear bud. Apple has a new retuned driver here and it is a good one. The sound is natural and has good definition. You will feel the bass and highs are crispy. It is hard to define how much better these are from the 2’s but they seem louder and have more clarity. A lot of this probably has to do with the new seal these give you in your ears as well (more on that later) but these are well tuned and are noticeably improved. This was an area that needed to improve as most other high end buds were better. Sony, Technics, and Bose were all better sounding at similar prices. Some of them offer higher end codecs for more lossless streams which Apple appears to still be avoiding. I still think there are better sounding buds out there but Apple has closed the gap in meaningful ways. Well done. Read more Read more

product_name: Apple AirPods Pro 3 Wireless Earbuds, Active Noise Cancellation, Live Translation, Heart Rate Sensing, Hearing Aid Feature, Bluetooth Headphones, Spatial Audio, High-Fidelity Sound, USB-C Charging
date: Reviewed in the United States on January 16, 2026
title: 5 stars, but hedge your expectations against Apple audio products
body: Best AirPods I’ve ever owned. I needed these for Spaces on X (Twitter), and the audio quality is the most pristine of all AirPods I’ve had. I am a bit of an audiophile, so they are not necessarily the “highest tier” audio quality you could purchase for this price. If you want something better for the price without caring if whether or not they are AirPods, you’re better-off looking at anything from Sennheiser. Thus, the value for the money is absolutely as inflated as you could imagine for a typical Apple product. I’ve noticed that these fit and stay in my ears much better than previous AirPods I’ve owned before. What actually makes these special is the ability to set up realistic spatial sound. If you take the time to set it up when you get these, you will be very happy with how insanely immersive these are. You turn your head in any direction, and you will hear the audio as if you are near it, and you will hear through one ear a little more than the other as you move your head in different directions. It’s a genius implementation for the purpose of immersive audio, and I love it very much. This can be shut off in Settings if you don’t want this feature. I should mention that this only occurs for the Music app (I’m not sure about Spotify or other streaming platforms, so DYOR), and not for other audio sources like YouTube or X Spaces. If you can afford this premium price, I would say go ahead and get them. However, it really needs to be said again that Apple product prices are inherently inflated. I have no intention of “testing” durability at all whatsoever because I ordered a shell for the AirPods case, but I will say that you definitely do not want to drop AirPods on hard surfaces. I dropped one AirPod several years ago when I owned the first generation, and that AirPod broke. Treat them with delicacy, because they generally are not very durable. Stay safe by purchasing AppleCare coverage for these AirPods. If you are already willing to spend this much on earbuds, then you have no excuse to not buy the coverage. Read more Read more

body: I bought these to replace my Nothing Ear headphones, they were ruined by a run or two through the wash. I was able to test them in a loud environment the other day when I was operating a garden tractor to plow my drive, they weren't able to cancel out the very loud tractor, but they did reduce the sound significantly. I did also have these headphones washed by my wife, and they made it through one wash cycle with no noticeable change to the performance. I did pay for Apple care for these since I know I'm prone to leaving things in my pockets and I know my wife doesn't check pockets. Hopefully I'll never have to use that, but we'll see. The headphones were completely dead after being washed and I did have to recharge them. Read more Read more

body: I'm a relatively new e-ink convert, having bought a tricked-out Kindle Scribe exactly two years ago (when the device was brand new). Long story short? I loved that device... Literally, to death... After dropping it on terrazzo floors in SEVERAL airports world-wide, not to mention the myriad spills at home on more forgiving carpet. In fact, I loved it so much that when the 11th gen Scribe went on fire-sale a few weeks ago, I bought the cheapest version to replace my original (recently non-functional) unit, simply swapping my old premium pen for the basic one. Clearly, I loved the device enough to buy it again... I adore the ability to mark up .pdf's, the note-taking capabilities and that even with this extra functionality, it's still a very limited device that allows me to focus on reading first. What I don't love, and never have? The weight. It's heavy to the point that I never used it if I couldn't prop it on something. Which led to it being dropped. A lot. Which led to the original unit failing. Not that it wasn't pretty sturdy... I mean, it survived repeated collisions with very unforgiving surfaces. It even weathered some relatively large liquid spills on and near it despite not being water resistant. Now? I just use the Scribe when I need to mark up .pdf's (like, when I'm gaming... I can keep my character sheets on the Kindle), or need something that can display larger printed materials... Like RPG basic books or comics. Hence, the new Kindle Paperwhite gen 12... product_name: Amazon Kindle Paperwhite 16GB (newest model) – 20% faster, with new 7" glare-free display and weeks of battery life – Black
date: Reviewed in the United States on May 31, 2025
title: Hold A Thousand Books in Your Hand
body: Imagine holding a thousand books in your hand. Imagine wearing your jammies while getting that next book in your series, and starting into it a minute later. Imagine reading at night in bed without needing a lamp because your book is backlit (and the light doesn’t hurt your eyes). Imagine having your kindle read to you (even if you don’t have audiobooks) with text to speech while you drive or fold laundry. The 12th edition of the Kindle Paperwhite is a fabulous device and well-worth considering if you read a lot. It’s not too big, not heavy, waterproof, and easy to carry in your purse or backpack. Note that if you get a cover with it (highly recommended), when you open the cover the Kindle turns on automatically to the last page you were reading. With the cover open it feels like you’re holding a thin book -- or fold back the cover if you prefer. The Kindle E-reader is not a computer. It’s not a tablet or a phone. It is a gizmo that acts like a book with a black-and-white flat e-ink screen showing typed sentences and paragraphs. You turn pages one at a time, just like a book. There is no movement on the page and no distraction or advertisement*. It is just text, plus any images that the author has included to illustrate his book. *NOTE: The advertisements on the Kindle are on the main page not the individual book pages and just show books you might like. They are unobtrusive. Read more Read more

date: Reviewed in the United States on April 19, 2026
title: Fast easy set up, smooth streaming
body: This is my 6th Amazon Fire TV stick. We have them on 3 TV's in our home and they get some pretty heavy use, so after a couple of years, I have to buy new ones because the old ones either stop working or slow down tremendously. This is a newer version and has HD quality, Alexa voice , pre- installed apps and takes just minutes to set up. This one I purchased to replace the one on my daughters TV in her room and I was about to set it up for her (she's 12) when she says, "I got this mom". "Ok great just let me know if you need help getting it set up and paired because sometimes the volume doesn't Always pair properly on first try". I go about my house work expecting to hear my daughters voice yell "Mom, can you help me"? Never came. My 12 year old set it up all on her own, no help from mom. That is how easy it is to set up the fire TV Stick. Albeit my daughter is a bright gifted Honor Roll student, lol. Seriously though you don't need to be tech savvy to set this up or use it. It's got Alexa voice built in so you can just press the blue button on the remote and ask Alexa to find a program for you. I've noticed zero buffering, connects to our wifi fast and easy and loads apps quickly. Even with our smart TVs, having a fire TV Stick is a plus. But if you have an older TV or an older smart TV that doesn't update anymore, this is the way to go. The design is easy to understand and flows smoothly. Definitely worth every penny. While I was ordering this one I read that Amazon will even take your old fire sticks that don't work anymore. Since I've got a few of those lying around, I think I'll check into it. For $20 , we get several years out of the fire stick so it's worth it to me. Read more Read more

date: Reviewed in the United States on March 21, 2026
title: Solid daily smartwatch that fits real life
body: I bought this smartwatch to get a clearer picture of my sleep, heart activity, and workouts without pulling out my phone all day. The sleep score and health monitoring sounded helpful, and the option to take ECG readings whenever I want and track blood oxygen felt reassuring for daily use. Day to day it works smoothly. The always on display is easy to glance at, and the watch stays comfortable for all day wear and even sleep. It handles workouts with solid metrics, and the band stays secure during runs. Battery life is up to 24 hours of normal use, and a quick 15 minute charge can add several hours. What stood out is how the insights feel actionable rather than just numbers. The alerts for high or low heart rate show up when I need them, and the sleep score lines up with how rested I feel. The durable glass plus a 50 meter water resistance makes it feel ready for everyday life, and safety features like fall detection and automatic check-in when I reach a destination give me extra peace of mind. One small caveat is that the size is a bit bulky on smaller wrists and the wealth of features can take a little time to navigate without checking the app often. It also depends on having it paired with a phone for some functions. Still, in everyday use it feels accurate and reliable, and the overall experience is genuinely satisfying. Overall, it's earned a five-star impression. Read more Read more

date: Reviewed in the United States on February 26, 2026
title: Sounds Great — But I Miss the Audio Jack
body: I’ve owned several Echo Dots over the years, and the newest model definitely sounds better than the older ones. The audio is fuller, clearer, and surprisingly punchy for such a small speaker. For casual music, podcasts, and general Alexa use, it does exactly what it’s supposed to do. Setup is simple, Alexa responsiveness is solid, and it integrates easily into a smart home setup. As a compact speaker for a bedroom, office, or kitchen, it’s a nice upgrade in sound quality compared to earlier generations. That said — I really hate that Amazon removed the 3.5mm audio jack on the latest models. That was a genuinely useful feature. Being able to hardwire it into better speakers or an amp gave you flexibility. Now you’re locked into Bluetooth or the internal speaker, which feels like a step backward for people who actually use their equipment beyond basic voice commands. If you just want a standalone smart speaker with better sound than older Dots, it’s a solid choice. But if you relied on the audio-out jack like I did, be aware it’s gone — and that’s disappointing.

date: Reviewed in the United States on August 14, 2023
title: Another great smart home purchase, to say the least.
body: My previous Echo Dot, purchased back in 2018, was a third-generation. It was a white shuffleboard stone covered in cloth and it is amazing. I'll never say it's not; in fact it's so amazing that I passed it on to my mom and she enjoys it a great deal. So, my main motivation for purchasing this, the fifth generation issued in 2022 – aside from familiarity with the brand – was I needed a Matter hub so my then-incoming Nanoleaf smart lightbulbs could work with my voice. But of course it is so much more. First thing I see is that the Echo Dot is no longer a white shuffleboard stone but is a smaller, navy blue sphere. Aside from being my favorite color, and thus very pleasing to look at, I find it that it is not practical any longer to mount on my wall because of its increased focus on the forward firing speaker; if I had mounted it, it would not be effective in spreading out to my entire room – the top of the speaker is reserved for the buttons which were present on the third-generation: volume up, volume down, action/wake and mute microphone, which leave no room for a speaker in that area. So it sits pretty on my side table facing the rest of my room, and does fine. First thing I hear is the sound quality is so much better than the third-generation Dot. The third-generation was okay in that regard, but it tended to sound a little overexposed when the volume was up high. Now, I don't find myself connecting via Bluetooth to my sound bar nearly as much because the speaker quality is just that good. The quiet parts of music are quiet and calming the way they should be, but the bass thumps quite effectively, surprisingly for such a small and lightweight speaker. Read more Read more

body: I’ve been using the Firestick 4K for a while now and overall, I'm quite pleased with its performance and ease of use. If you're considering this device and can find it on sale for about $25, it's worth it for the streaming capabilities, but be prepared to navigate a frustrating remote experience.

Worked right away volume and everything! It doesn't get any better or easier than that!! It is a genuine Roku remote and is very heavy duty. I think it will last forever as long as no one loses it! Overall, it's an excellent value and product! Will buy again if necessary!

product_name: JBL Vibe Beam - True Wireless JBL Deep Bass Sound Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.2, Water & Dust Resistant, Hands-Free Call with VoiceAware, Up to 32 Hours of Battery Life (Black)
date: Reviewed in the United States on October 21, 2023
title: Top quality audio after a few adjustments
body: This review is about the audio features of the JBL Vibe Beam earbuds. I'm an audio engineer so audio quality is very important to me. As such, I use wired connections as much as possible to maintain the integrity of the audio signal. However, many newer consumer-level products no longer have audio jacks, so I finally decided to invest in some wireless earbuds as a backup to the other earphones and headphones that I use. I didn't want to spend a lot of money as I didn't foresee myself using this product very much, and since this is my first foray into wireless audio earphones, if they were just ok or they broke after a while, then I wouldn't feel too bad about the little money that I spent. Also, I wanted to focus on finding wireless earbuds with excellent audio quality first as I already have an excellent high-end bluetooth earpiece for cell phone use, and I wouldn't be using this product as a bluetooth headset for my phone. After doing much research, I chose the JBL Vibe Beam's as they were at my price point, I'm familiar with JBL products, and I could reasonably expect that they were likely deliver the high audio quality that I've come to expect from JBL products. In my research, I read reviews that complained about the harshness of the high end with high frequency instruments like cymbals or the lack of bass, so I was a bit concerned. Read more Read more

product_name: JBL Vibe Beam - True Wireless JBL Deep Bass Sound Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.2, Water & Dust Resistant, Hands-Free Call with VoiceAware, Up to 32 Hours of Battery Life (Black)
date: Reviewed in the United States on April 7, 2026
title: Easy to use
body: The JBL Vibe Beam earbuds are truly impressive for their price. The sound is clear with deep, punchy bass that JBL is known for, making music more enjoyable. They fit comfortably in the ears, even during movement or daily activities. The battery life is solid, and the connection is stable without interruptions. Overall, they offer great value and are an excellent choice if you're looking for quality wireless earbuds without spending too much.

product_name: JBL Vibe Beam - True Wireless JBL Deep Bass Sound Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.2, Water & Dust Resistant, Hands-Free Call with VoiceAware, Up to 32 Hours of Battery Life (Black)
date: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2026
title: Excellent product for the price
body: For the price point, these earbuds have been awesome!! Instead of getting overly expensive earbuds that I might lose or not like, I bought these as starter Bluetooth earbuds and haven't opted for anything else since. I mainly use them to watch shows or listen to music (especially when walking). I sometimes use them for phone calls. For me, the sound on calls is clear but I have gotten the feedback that it can sometimes be loud with background noise for the person on the other end of the line. I have small ears and the buds are a surprisingly comfortable fit. I did separately buy a silicone lanyard that I use every time with the earbuds so that I don't lose them. Without the lanyard, I am confident I would have lost the buds. The charging capacity is also great. The buds last a long time before needing to be charged. The control buttons on the buds are very easy to use and work reliably. These sync well to my phone and tablet but I've had issues syncing them to airplane screens. I also bought a silicone case for it, which has been useful.

product_name: JBL Vibe Beam - True Wireless JBL Deep Bass Sound Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.2, Water & Dust Resistant, Hands-Free Call with VoiceAware, Up to 32 Hours of Battery Life (Black)
date: Reviewed in the United States on October 25, 2025
title: JBL Vibe Beam vs Soundcore Liberty 4 NC vs Soundcore P40i
body: I ordered 3 different earbuds all within a similar price range(+\- $30) to figure out which one I liked the most - Soundcore Liberty 4 NC, P40i, and JBL Vibe Beam. Here are my notes and comparisons. The Liberty 4 NC is my favorite but there is one aspect I don’t like. They Liberty’s are very clear with good bass and their app allows you to customize EQ settings which is deal breaker for me. (All three pairs have custom EQs within their respective apps). The EQ settings and customization from the JBL app are far superior as they give you unlimited options. The JBL Vibe was less comfortable but more so than JBL Tune(ordered these a while back, the Tunes fall out constantly!). The JBL Vibes are not noise canceling and I knew that before hand so I’m not knocking it. The JBLs are fine for the price point but not the best for quality as they use 8mm drivers vs 11mm drivers for the Soundcore. The one thing I don’t like with the Liberty’s is the slightest white noise I get when between songs or silent parts. The P40i’s and Vibes don’t have that problem. The P40i and JBLs have great bass and fit well but they lack in higher frequencies and that’s important for audiophiles. The P40i also have a much friendlier app interface with many more preset features. BUT the Liberty’s have an option to set up your “ideal listening EQ” and that is a great feature as it adjusts to your preferred sound through samples and takes the guess work out. product_name: JBL Vibe Beam - True Wireless JBL Deep Bass Sound Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.2, Water & Dust Resistant, Hands-Free Call with VoiceAware, Up to 32 Hours of Battery Life (Black)
date: Reviewed in the United States on April 12, 2026
title: Survived washer and dryer cycle!!
body: Guess who I bought this for? Yes, the husband. Guess who did the laundry and forgot to empty the pockets and left these JBL ear buds in his jeans pockets? Yes the husband. The sound quality of these are nice, especially for the price point. But what blew me away was what happened last week. It got thrown into a regular wash cycle and then a high heat dry cycle and survived and still works! Well, one of them worked right away.. the other one, took two days but works like normal, I guess it had to dry out? Yes, they were in the charging case when they were washed, but bothe the ear buds and charging case still works perfectly fine! I guess they were telling the truth when they said "water resistant". I bought another pair anyway because I thought the other earbud was never going to work, well I was wrong! The second order is worth keeping as this might be the Nokia of earbuds, lol! *If you know what I'm talking about, you're my people, lol 😆) Happy husband said "see, nothing to worry about, they were water resistant".. says the man who washed his earbuds.. I'm glad JBL offers good quality earbuds, highly recommend!

title: Decent, but far from perfect (for me).
body: I've had these for nearly two months, using them at my job working with dogs. Typically, I listen to long-form videos on YouTube or music while working outside in a yard full of dogs. Having come from using some relatively basic ear buds bought at Target, I wanted something a little more durable and with stronger sound. I found these JBL buds for a decent price and decided to go for it, knowing that just about anything from JBL would sound better than what I had. All in all, they sounded great for the price and I enjoyed the easy touch controls for the most part, but there were some significant problems with how I was trying to use them on the job. For one thing, I'd never had ear buds in this design, with an Air Pod style handle or whatever and I worried that it would be unwieldy. Fortunately, it actually made getting them out of my ear or out of my pocket easier - so that was good. However, the touch controls were a little too sensitive for me, as I'd often find myself fumbling to grab one of the buds from a pocket and accidentally hit that touch spot in the process, either restarting my media or skipping onto something else entirely. date: Reviewed in the United States on January 7, 2026
title: Reliable six outlet surge protector for a tidy workspace
body: I bought this to replace a tangle of cords cluttering my desk. With six AC outlets and three USB ports, it handles my laptop charger, monitor, lamp, speakers, and a couple of phone chargers all at once. I looked for solid surge protection and the 1680 joules rating, plus the ETL listing, and it checked those boxes. The outlets are widely spaced, so I can fit bulky adapters without blocking neighbors. A six foot cord gives me flexibility to place it where outlets are easy to reach. In daily use it sits on my desk, and I can reach everything easily without bending down. The USB ports charge steadily while the main device stays plugged in, with two USB-A ports up to 2.4A each and a USB-C port up to 3A for faster charging. The three ports total 15W. The flat six foot plug and the included cable ties help keep the setup neat and the cord tucked away. What stood out is how quickly protection kicks in when there is a surge and how quiet it is in normal use. The wide voltage design means I can use it in spaces with different outlets without worrying about compatibility. The six outlets stay accessible even with larger plugs in use, and the overall build feels solid enough for everyday use. One small caveat is that when all three USB ports are drawing power, the total charging speed can dip a bit, but I still get reliable everyday charging. Other than that minor note, the experience is consistently smooth and confident. Overall I am happy with this unit and would rate it five stars for keeping my devices safe and organized. date: Reviewed in the United States on April 7, 2026
title: Accurate Sizing, Helpful Trial Period, and Worth It Before Buying the Ring
body: I purchased the Oura Ring 4 sizing kit because I didn’t want to guess on sizing for a $300+ health device, and I’m very glad I didn’t skip this step. The sizing kit is well-designed and closely mimics the look and feel of the actual ring. Oura recommends wearing a test ring for at least 24 hours. Still, I found it helpful to wear mine for several days to account for normal fluctuations in finger size (sleep, activity, temperature, etc.). This made a noticeable difference in choosing the correct size. One of the biggest benefits is accuracy. I typically wear a size 7 in rings, but after testing, I found a size 6 fit best for the Oura Ring. Without the kit, I likely would have ordered the wrong size. The fit of the actual ring matched the sizer exactly. I also appreciated being able to test the ring during real-life conditions. I wore it during work shifts that involved frequent handwashing, hand sanitizer use, and repeated glove changes, and it held up well. That gave me confidence in both the sizing and the practicality of wearing the ring daily. Another advantage is saving time. Purchasing the sizing kit separately allowed me to determine my size right away and order the ring without waiting for the standard sizing process (which can add a couple of weeks). For anyone eager to receive their ring sooner, this is a helpful option. Read more Read more

It comes with a short HDMI extender, which is a great touch because it makes using a "stick" form device such as this not interfere with being in the way of your other HDMI ports. Make sure you use the power adapter it came with (or a USB adapter capable of outputting 2 Amps) because you will run in to problems quickly if it doesn't detect enough amperage. I rather wish it came with a longer cable, as it is not uncommon these days for a TV to be wall mounted, but that's just my own opinion. Setup is very easily done and has nice guidance every step of the way. Even getting the remote to work with my TV was super easy and included in the first time setup guidance. The remote ended up only being able to control my TV's power and volume, which is not a big deal, and I'm sure most individuals who buy this intend to use it as the main source of media to play on their TV. body: Yes, my subject line sounds like that trash hyperbole you get on annoying pop-up ads on your Smartphone! And ironically, it usually has to do with ear buds. No… the reason for all this hallelujah chorus, is that I’m really totally both jazzed, and a bit in awe of these earbuds. I think the main mind-blowing thing about them is the price. date: Reviewed in the United States on March 24, 2026
title: Good Wireless Earphones Option for Price
body: Works well compared to similar products. For around two years I was using a similar, different brand of wireless earphones also purchased off of Amazon, and they worked okay. I had originally switched over to wireless because my old headphone jack finally stopped working, unfortunately; now, of course, my new phone doesn't even have a headphone jack in the first place. I started buying these when I was no longer able to purchase from the previous brand off of Amazon. They were both around the same price and looked relatively similar. After a few months of using these, I can say that these are better than my previous brand, which I will refer as P.B. for short moving forward. As an avid music listener, these get the job done. For starters, they pair to my phone much faster (3 seconds max) as opposed to P.B. (7-12 seconds), they're also much better at keeping debris, junk, and other foreign materials out of the inner parts; I'd have to buy new earphones from P.B. every 3-5 months for this very reason. Perhaps my biggest compliment is the fact that the interactive "buttons" on the top sides of these earphones are far less sensitive than P.B. I'm personally not a huge fan of these buttons in the first place, but I can confidently say that I've accidentally pressed those sensors on these earphones relatively far less (maybe 2 or 3 times a week) then P.B. (at least 10 times a week). The case is also pretty durable; if I were to have dropped P.B.'s case with the earphones inside, there would have been a 100% chance that they would have gone flying; these, however, from the few times I've dropped the case, have stayed inside the case, undamaged. Battery life for both is about the same (about 4-5 hours but haven't really noticed a whole lot), as is/was audio quality. Overall a good product for the price; my only complaint is with the occasional, accidental, button press, pausing my music, which can't be disabled as far as a know. Frustrating, as this was a problem that I had with P.B. as well, but not a deal breaker. For casual music listing, this shouldn't be a problem. Originally had to return my first pair for issues with the hinge on the case breaking, but was sent a new product at no cost and haven't had any problems with the new one. 10/10 for price, 8/10 for audio quality, and 7.5/10 for battery life. title: Ottime cuffie in-ear economiche
body: La qualità dell'audio è molto buona per il prezzo, come da aspettativa per JLab. Packaging spartano, ma contenente due dimensioni alternative (oltre a quelli già installati) di gommini per adattarsi a varie dimensioni di condotti uditivi. L'autonomia è buona, non eccezionale, mi durano circa tre/quattro ore in videochiamata. La custodia esterna consente diverse ricariche complete. Le tempistiche di ricarica degli auricolari nella base sono accettabili, un po' meno quelle della custodia. Molto comodo il cavo USB-C integrato, che consente una ricaricara comoda anche con un telefono o un power bank dotato di tale uscita. La funzione Be Aware è utile e molto efficace per rumori vicini. Personalmente mi è stato possibile condurre una breve conversazione senza rimuovere le cuffie, ma è meno efficace con rumori distanti o quando si riproducono contenuti a volume alto o medio-alto. Comoda l'applicazione, di cui utilizzo principalmente la limitazione del volume a 80 dB. Mi ritengo generalmente molto soddisfatto dell'acquisto.

date: Reviewed in France on August 9, 2025
title: Great value, reliable everyday earbuds
body: I’ve been using the JLab Go Air Pop+ for over a month now, and for the price, they’re incredibly reliable. The sound quality is good—not on the same level as my Bose headphones, of course—but perfectly fine for casual music listening, phone calls, and especially audiobooks. The earbuds are small and lightweight to the point that I sometimes forget I’m wearing them. I’ve even fallen asleep with them on and woke up without any ear discomfort. The companion app is a nice bonus, allowing you to customize tap controls and tweak EQ settings. Battery life is impressive: after 3 hours of audiobook listening, I still had over 40% left, and the case charges them quickly. The integrated charging cable on the case is a thoughtful touch—it saves you from hunting for a cable. If I had to nitpick, the case feels quite light; a little extra heft would make it feel more premium. But given the price point, that’s hardly a dealbreaker. Overall, an excellent 5-star buy for anyone looking for affordable, comfortable, and functional earbuds.

product_name: Blink Video Doorbell (newest model) – Head-to-toe HD view, two-year battery life, and simple setup. Required Sync Module not included – Add-On (Black)
date: Reviewed in the United States on March 8, 2026
title: High Quality Video and Audio
body: The Blink Doorbell is quick and easy to set up. First, scan the QR code inside the Doorbell with your phone before physically setting it up. This will link it with your system, making theft of the Doorbell useless. Once activated, it will only work with your account-specific system. Placement: Optimal Height is generally 40–48 inches from the ground. This is the typical height of a doorbell. You can buy an optional no-drill Blink Vinyl Siding Clip on Etsy to avoid putting holes in your siding. The clip slides under and between the siding. Intercom: to speak to someone through the Blink Outdoor 2K camera, open the “live view” in the phone app and then tap the microphone icon (unmute), at the screen bottom. The mic icon will turn green, allowing you to speak through your phone. Tap the mic icon again to mute, allowing the person at the Doorbell to speak. The audio on the Blink Doorbell operates like a walkie-talkie; only one person can speak at a time. There may be a 1-2 second delay. Night Vision: works fine, showing a clear view of the immediate surroundings. Also check that your phone’s Blink app has permission to use the microphone. Go to the phone settings > apps > blink > permissions > microphone > and select allowed. That’s all there is to it. This is an easy to set up, effective security device. Read more Read more

Not only is the video on the Blink doorbell much sharper but it’s a wider angle that takes in my entire front yard - granted it’s a small urban front yard - and most of the front porch as well. Setting it up in the Blink app was simple as I already have some Blink cameras on the property. I set the ring setting in the Alexa app and it’s integrating flawlessly. Glad I made the switch. product_name: Blink Video Doorbell (newest model) – Head-to-toe HD view, two-year battery life, and simple setup. Required Sync Module not included – Add-On (Black)
date: Reviewed in the United States on March 3, 2026
title: Clear images, local storage, with minor issues
body: We had been using a different brand video doorbell, but decided we didn't want to continue to pay an annual fee and have our videos stored in the cloud. This doorbell allows us to store up to 256gb of video in a flash drive in the sync module. It works well, but there are a few issues with it. It advertised that it can be hard wired which is true. However, that wiring only connects it to our physical doorbell ringer, allowing it to ring when the button is pushed. It still runs on batteries that need to be replaced eventually. Our last doorbell was hard wired with no batteries to worry about. There's also no way to set it up to work with a chime when it detects motion. It can use a Blink Mini camera as a chime, but that, or any other 3rd party chime, only work when the button is pushed. The only alert for motion is from the app. Our last doorbell had a chime that alerted us any time there was motion. When it does detect motion and we click on the notification to open the app, it shows a blank screen saying a recording is in progress. We either have to wait until that recording is done to see what the motion was, or click open live view to see it in real time. It should automatically show what's being recorded when the app is opened. Read more Read more

title: Good headphones for the price, some minor cons.
body: TLDR: good quality and sound for price, minor inconveniences in setup. ANC mode works well. These cause my ears to get very hot quickly, so if you have sensory issues this might be a dealbreaker. These headphones have good sound, especially for the relatively low price. The app is not user friendly, but not really required to use the item. The extremely tiny "user manual" included in the box is impossible to read if you are over 40, so you will need to scan the QR code to get directions online. All that being said, they are pretty easy to pair to IPhone. Also the noise cancellation is superb! My husband was sitting right next to me talking and I couldn't hear him at all with the headphones on in ANC mode. Battery seems good, I don't know for sure if these will auto shut off, but after a day or so of non-use you will need to re-connect in Bluetooth settings. Have used near water, so I can't address water resistance, but I bought them for an international flight soon, time will tell about durability as it's only been about a week so far.

date: Reviewed in the United States on November 25, 2025
title: Great general purpose headset - good for comfort, audio and battery life
body: This is the one to get, for general purpose Bluetooth headsets. The combination of bone conduction and speakers in front of your ears, not pointed into them, works great. My previous one was a single ear, in-ear type, which became annoying and uncomfortable. This one is comfortable and doesn't get in the way of environmental sound to my ear. The voice on the phone just appears in my head. It's great being able to work with my hands while I talk on the phone or listen to music, podcasts, etc. Music - I'm a sound engineer, and this is fine for casual music listening. You're not going to get deep bass or audiophile detail in music, and it's no substitute for good closed-back headphones, but it's mostly balanced and good enough. I use the Standard setting in the app. The Volume Boost setting doesn't seem to do much at all. The Classic setting, which is bone-conduction only and takes more battery power, doesn't sound as good. I tried other EQ settings, including the 5 band custom one, and I usually stay with Standard. If you need to use the bass or treble boost settings, there's probably something wrong with the source audio. Volume - music volume maxes out just a little before I wish it did. It's not super loud, but it's plenty in a quiet environment. Using it on a bicycle or with significant wind, you'd probably want to put a headband over it, and even then, it may not be loud enough. For podcasts or phone calls, volume is better; no issues with it not being loud enough yet. Read more Read more

body: Super happy with these. A bit on the pricey side, but totally worth it in the long run. Multi device pairing is a breeze too, only being made easier by the app where you can toggle on and off devices that the headphones will try to pair to. So, basically you can pair a bunch of devices and turn the ability to connect to them on and off. For me, I usually pair to my laptop and phone, but have my watch and streaming stick saved and toggled off so the headphones prioritize my phone and laptop so they don't fight for pairing power. I had the minus volume button deteriorate and come off rendering the rocker almost useless. I reached out to support and they sent me a new pair free of charge since it was still under warranty. Really nice and easy and now I have two pairs, just one I can't turn the volume down on nor rely on its water resistance. I'd consider myself an average sized dude 5'10" @160lbs and the "mini" size fits perfect and I barely know it's on me until I get a notification and hear it IN STEREO! Stereo audio is also a big plus for me and one of the main downsides to using just one in-ear earbud. Plus I get normal surround sound of the world around me in addition to stereo music. title: Throw Away All Your Old Headsets - THIS IS THE ONE YOU WANT!
body: The first thing I need to say is just get it!! This is such an awesome product and it is worth the price. The quality and functionality are superb, plus I love the simplicity because it's so easy to operate with the multi-function button. The sound is outstanding and the app gives you the capability to increase the base, the treble and overall volume among other things. These bone conduction headphones are the only way to go because your ears are left uncovered so you can hear other things going on around you, but at the same time doesn't really detract from the music you're listening to or even a phone conversation. Speaking of phone conversations, the built-in microphone is excellent and your phone calls are clear and crisp without any outside interference, whether you're talking or listening. This feature is just as good or better than the overall sound quality when music is playing. I was especially drawn to this device because I have an issue with the anatomy of my ears. For whatever reason I could never get earbuds to stay in my ears. Much as I tried they would always fall out which was extremely frustrating. Finally my doctor told me I fell into about 10% of the population that has this same problem, so the only solution for me was the over ear style. One last thing I need to mention is the sizing. Specifically the standard versus the mini. I'm a male, 5'11" and weigh 180lbs with basically a normal head and short hair. Based on some other reviews I got the mini and I'm glad I did. It's a perfect fit without too much play in the back of my head. I can easily move my head up, down and all around and the headphones stay in place with a great deal of comfort. Therefore, I would only recommend the standard size to someone with a larger head or someone with a lot of hair in the back of their head because you need the extra space provided by the standard size. date: Reviewed in the United States on October 20, 2024
title: Excellent clarity and blazing speed; a worthy upgrade.
body: The screen has excellent sharpness and contrast, the best of any ereader I've ever seen in person. It brings to mind another device that featured strikingly good sharpness and contrast despite the layers over the screen: The Voyage. I don't have one on hand to do a direct comparison, but it certainly seems to have that same sort of secret sauce going for it. I'll be very interested to see some direct comparisons from people who still have a Voyage on hand. It's not as apparent in photos, but I'm surprised at how much sharper it is than my 2021 PW. I knew from the early reviews that it would be better, but I wasn't expecting it to be so noticeable. Even my beloved Oasis comes up short in a direct comparison! Much has been made of the speed of this iteration of the PW, and it's not just empty hype. I never thought the word "fast" would ever really apply to an e-ink screen due to the limitations of the technology, but the Kindle team has made that a reality. Pages turn as quickly as I can tap the screen, and while I've never cried out to the heavens about page turns being too slow, it's impressive to see such responsiveness on an e-ink screen. The true benefits of this speed come into play when navigating menus, adjusting settings, and especially when typing. I honestly did not think it was possible for an e-ink screen to be so fast and responsive. This is truly impressive. The speed in itself is not a reason to upgrade to this model, but if you're already considering an upgrade, it is definitely an extra enticement. Read more Read more

title: Limitations beyond sideloading, but probably still the future.
body: Preface: I've used FireTV devices for quite a while now, and have learned to appreciate what separates them from their competition, but also some of the annoying quirks that they struggle with sometimes. This device, with VegaOS manages to do away with most of the quirks, but also takes away some of the reason why I buy FireTV devices in the first place. Hardware: I'll get this out of the way quickly, since it's basically unchanged and is perhaps the least-impactful aspect of any Fire TV device. Yes, it has a micro-USB port for power, get over it. Amazon probably has a warehouse full of them, and for a connector that you plug in once in a device's life... it's not really impactful. body: I really like this surge protector and ended up buying a second one after owning my first for a while — that alone says a lot about the quality and value for money. The layout is excellent: 8 outlets, plus 3 USB ports including a USB-C, which still feels refreshingly modern. The plug-in capability is great, and charging phones and accessories is fast and reliable with good charging speed. It’s also very flexible in terms of versatility. I use it for everyday electronics, chargers, and desk setups, and the mountability makes it easy to keep things neat and off the floor if you want a cleaner setup. The only reason I didn’t give this 5 stars is power handling. If something with a higher draw gets plugged in — like a hairdryer — the unit will shut itself off. On one hand, I appreciate the safety aspect, but on the other hand, it means this isn’t ideal as a true all-purpose room surge protector for mixed appliances. Overall, it’s a well-designed, modern power strip that’s perfect for electronics and charging stations — just be mindful of its limits with higher-power devices.

title: Fantastic mount
body: This mount is excellent. It perfectly fits a standard 16 inch stud spacing, but no more than that. The outer most holes for the stud mount go out to only 16 1/4 inch, and have slotted holes for the smaller stud spacing. More specifically, I bought and mounted this for an LG G5 Series 55” OLED. Not only does it fit for this specific TV, but it also has room for adjusting it up and down, sliding left and right, and allows the TV to come out and inwards using the extendable hinges. I do like the little bubble level it comes with, it’s nice to keep and save in my office desk, for little nuance projects. Only thing I wish it came with, were 4 washers for the lag/stud bolts. The surface area that the bottom of the head of the bolt, doesn’t seem to have much contact area for my liking. So I added 4, 1 inch wide washers to give the bolt more area to clamp the mount and spread the load across the slotted hole. Other than that, with a little measuring and thought, this mount was extremely easy to install. Do take the time to see how far up or down you want mount the long black mounts that actually attach to the TV. Other than that, it’s still holding up great. Before I mounted my TV, I grabbed a bathroom scale, and while i stood on it, I pulled more of my weight onto the mount, and I wanted to confirm it would hold 100 or so pounds. It did so very well, felt and sounded sturdy at about 140 pounds. This manufacture does state 100 pound limit if I can remember correctly. So just keep that in mind.

title: Bon produit
body: Cette enceinte Bluetooth Anker SoundCore 2 est une option intéressante pour son prix. La qualité sonore est correcte pour une écoute d'appoint, et les basses sont étonnamment présentes pour sa taille compacte. L'autonomie de 24 heures est un vrai atout, idéale pour une journée entière sans se soucier de la recharge. La portée Bluetooth de 20 mètres et l'étanchéité IPX7 sont également des caractéristiques appréciables pour une utilisation en extérieur ou près de l'eau. Le microphone intégré est pratique pour les appels mains libres. Cependant, si vous recherchez une puissance sonore élevée pour animer une fête ou sonoriser un grand espace, cette enceinte risque de montrer ses limites. Elle est parfaite pour une écoute personnelle ou dans un petit groupe, mais ne vous attendez pas à un son qui remplisse une pièce. En résumé, un bon rapport qualité-prix pour une enceinte portable et polyvalente, mais la puissance sonore pourrait être un peu juste pour certains usages.

product_name: Soundcore by Anker P20i True Wireless Earbuds, 10mm Drivers with Big Bass, Bluetooth 5.3, 30H Long Playtime, Water-Resistant, 2 Mics for AI Clear Calls, 22 Preset EQs, Customization via App
date: Reviewed in the United States on April 17, 2026
title: Awesome ear set -- can't be beat at this price point
body: Outstanding! For the low price (~$20), this is an amazing ear set. I have been using Apple Airpods and then SamSung Buds for the past 6 years. As expected, this set does not have all the features like Apple's or Samsung's, but having used this set for several months, now I am wondering if I ever really needed those features (for a price of $150-200!). Great sound, long battery, light but solid build quality, coupled with no fear of losing! The only thing is that when I drop it on the floor, it opens up. Each pod is made of two pieces, which separate as it hits the floor. After having to click them back together several times, I added superglue before click them back on both sides. Now they don't open up. Definitely recommend this set.

product_name: Soundcore by Anker P20i True Wireless Earbuds, 10mm Drivers with Big Bass, Bluetooth 5.3, 30H Long Playtime, Water-Resistant, 2 Mics for AI Clear Calls, 22 Preset EQs, Customization via App
date: Reviewed in the United States on April 17, 2026
title: best buds for the price. look no further.
body: I never write reviews, but I have to for this product. Just as most have said, these are AWESOME. I have had nothing but bad experiences with other wireless earbuds so I stayed away from them. But as my over the ear headphones of 8 years stopped working, I had to buy new headphones so I decided to give them another chance. I kept hearing good things about Soundcore so I trusted the reviews and they live up to the hype completely. The audio quality of course isn't the best, but it's clear enough, loud enough, and has enough fidelity not just for the pricepoint, but in my honest opinion exceeds it. But the most important thing of all, connectivity. That is the number one factor when looking for wireless earbuds. Many earbuds have decent quality, but if they don't stay connected, who cares. These always stay connected. Previous earbuds, the right would connect but not the left. This is because with other earbuds the right is the primary connected device, while the left is connected to the right bud. With these, it seems both buds are connected simultaneously. Also, these aren't just good for music and movies, even in games they don't lose their synchronicity. Only one problem, when I tried playing a PC game and watching a movie at the same time, the buds did cause significant lag and input delays in my game, but that is totally forgiveable. When the buds are dedicated just t your game and not to another app they work very decently. Most wireless earbuds I tried in the past always had this problem of synchronizing to games. I am absolutely blown away at the quality of these for the price. The biggest downside I have heard is they only last about a year before they stop being functional. But still, for only $20 these are unbeatable if you're on a budget. I recommend the a20i over the p20i as the bass is a little too loud, but you can easily fix this by downloading the soundcore app and choosing the "reduce bass" EQ setting. Also, these are bluetooth only. They don't utilize a USB dongle. So if you have an older laptop or desktop that doesn't have bluetooth, you will need to purchase either a bluetooth dongle or a bluetooth/wifi PCI-E card for your desktop. Read more Read more

date: Reviewed in the United States on March 30, 2026
title: They’re fine but not my favorite.
body: I gave these ear pods three stars because I really haven’t had big issues with them - they are just a basic, mediocre set of ear buds in my opinion. I bought these to use until I can afford new AirPods. They are fine, but there are definitely things I dislike about them. The sound quality is just ok, and sometimes it’ll do this thing where the sound goes in and out. That doesn’t happen often, thankfully. Also, they do not stop playing the audio if you take them out of your ears and don’t put them right in the case. My least favorite part, though, is the way they fit in my ears. I have small ears, so I have a hard time finding ear buds that won’t work their way out of my ears as I wear them. These ones are too big and when I touch them to adjust them back into my ear, the touch control is initiated. All I’ve noticed the touch control doing, though, is making beeps when I touch the earbuds to adjust them. I’m not sure what else is supposed to happen, but I haven’t noticed anything changing when I touch the ear buds while I’m listening to them. It’s mostly just annoying. I also haven’t tried to swap out the rubber parts of the ear buds with smaller ones. I can’t remember if this set even came with replacements, and I don’t remember where mine are if they did come with them. So that may help the size issue if I were to find them.

date: Reviewed in the United States on January 7, 2026
title: Title: "Game-Changing Tile Tracker: How It Helped Me Recover My Stolen Wallet!"
body: Tile by Life360 Mate – Bluetooth Tracker (Green Surf) – My Personal Take When I say a product is worth five stars, I don’t hand it out lightly. For me, a 5-star rating means the item not only works as advertised but transcends expectations—like finding a lifehack you didn’t know you needed. The Tile Mate by Life360? It just earned that rare, well-earned five stars. Let me tell you why. Seamless Setup, Instant Peace of Mind First off, I was blown away by how easy it was to program these little devices. Hooking them up to my phone via the app took under two minutes—no complicated steps, no frustration. I slapped one on my keys, another in my wallet, and bam—instant confidence I’d never lose them again. The app lets you name each Tile, set reminders, and even get notifications when you leave your house without an item. For someone chronically bad at remembering where they put their keys, this is pure gold. Real-Life Lifesaver: Recovering My Stolen Wallet Fast-forward to the real test. I had the Tile Mate in my wallet when it was stolen at a restaurant. Panicked (and rightfully so—credit cards, ID, everything was inside), I launched the Tile app. The tracker pinged to the last known location, and using the “Community Find” feature (where thousands of Tile users anonymously help locate lost items), it showed me a nearby address. I flagged it to the police within 10 minutes, and—miracle of miracles—they tracked the thief down and recovered my wallet (minus the cash, of course). The cards? All intact. The relief? Priceless. body: Honestly kind of blown away by how much I like this thing. This is my first Mac and it's insane how much power is crammed into such a small form factor. Not only that, but just overall build quality, fit and finish, everything just feels very premium. I've had several Windows laptops and they all just feel pretty cheap, even at this same price point. That being said, this is Apple's "middle ground" laptop for a reason. If you plan on doing a lot of video editing or intensive gaming, just get the Pro. But everything shy of that runs amazingly well on this, and in a much more compact form factor. You can honestly still play plenty of games on the Air. But if you want to play FPS or something like Cyber Punk, definitely get a Pro. Lite stuff like Balatro, MTG Arena, Hearthstone, Stardew Valley, etc run perfectly on the Air. Also, if you plan on this being your one and only computer, get the 15 inch, a Pro, or connect it to a monitor. For portability, 13 inch is king. title: Impressive web PC, first time mac user, massive upgrade over a 4 year old Lenovo Yoga 13
body: The neo is really impressive for $600. It has a nice screen, an incredibly rigid aluminum chassis, loads web pages and videos super fast, boots quick. Impressive Bluetooth and WiFi range and performance (Bluetooth is so much better then my old laptop), good speakers(for a laptop), good battery life, a decent keyboard, excellent trackpad, decent web cam, looks nice, low heat output and no vents to get blocked, compact and light. Highly recommended for anyone shopping for a compact web machine. The only negatives on the hardware are: lack of keyboard backlight, the interior edge of the chassis is sharp, not enough to cut you, but enough to be annoying when typing in some positions, not many ports just a normal USB C and a 2.0 speed USB C (both can be used for charging) and screen does not fold flat, so the laptop cannot be stood up against the wall. I was a bit unsure about the Neo because of it using a cellphone chip, but that was not a problem at all. The Neo is super snappy, as fast as my Workstation/Gaming PC when loading web pages and videos. Also I have not noticed any issues with the amount of RAM, 8GB seems sufficient for running a web browser, just as long as you do not go crazy on tabs in Chrome or if you use Firefox that actually manages tabs properly. As a web browser machine I doubt that this will have any performance issues. I am sure that you would hit limits of the mobile CPU if you do did something serious like video editing, but it is more then enough for more casual use. Read more Read more

date: Reviewed in the United States on August 10, 2025
title: Pros and cons got me on the fence about keeping
body: So today is the last day before my return window closes and I am on the fence about keeping them. Straight up there are features I really like. But there are two major downsides that cause me to think twice. I have been wearing them everyday since the day I received them, for hours a day, in multiple scenarios : work, workout, yard work, housework, etc. Especially as I really wanted to give them a fair chance. Right from the get go just a few hours in they began hurting my ears. So I did what I usually do with other like-headphones: I turned them around. At first when I did this they seemed to be unidirectional but after a few times they became bidirectional and it really helps with the longevity of wearability, esp if I keep flipping all day on long days. But still nothing like my old headphones that I have had for years and paid $50 for, which have outlasted multiple other pairs. With my old ones I can go 8 hours before feeling the need to flip em around due to discomfort. But staying on the longevity side what I do love about them is the battery, which I can go three to four long days of wear without needing to recharge. Love love love that!! Esp when needing to run out of the house and didn’t have time to charge my headphones. Plus’s the ease of connecting to WiFi! Love!! Instead of having to go into systems and go thru the whole manual routine of connecting, these you just press the button and the screen pops up to connect! Plus they do let you know when you have a potential important message, whether text, email or fb and asks if you want it read. Then it seems to have smart capability to filter according to your interests. Even not to interrupt you again if you say no. One of the biggest drawbacks, though, esp when working in the heat, like yard work or cleaning out garage, is they cause my ears to sweat. And I’m typically not a sweater! Hate that! And they really sweat! They are attractive and I got the “pink,” which as some have said don’t look pink, except in certain lighting. Which honestly after using fur a month I do think of these as my “pink” headphones. If the ear cuff piece wasn’t so small they’d be perfect! Sound quality and all that is good but I’m not an expert on that, as I’m typically listening to audio books or podcasts and not necessarily music. And they did come in a really nice packaging. But so today I’m opting to wear my old headphones, which are finally, after ten years, starting to lose their charge-ability, but so that’ll be the test of whether I just need to look for another version of those or decide to keep the Beats Solo. I do like them but like a pair of shoes, you gotta have comfortably! Read more Read more

date: Reviewed in Canada on December 17, 2025
title: Iconic style, modern performance
body: The Beats Solo 4 represents a major technical leap for one of the most recognizable pairs of headphones on the planet. While the iconic, foldable design remains familiar, the internal hardware has been completely overhauled to meet modern standards. The Gold and Black aesthetic provides a high-end, sophisticated look that feels more mature than earlier iterations. One of the most impressive upgrades is the staggering 50 hours of battery life, which allows most people to go an entire week without reaching for a charging cable. Even if you do run out of power, the Fast Fuel feature provides five hours of playback from just a ten-minute charge, and the addition of a 3.5mm port means you can still listen to music even when the battery is completely dead. Sound quality has also seen a significant refinement. These headphones now feature custom 40mm transducers designed to reduce electronic interference and distortion, resulting in a much cleaner and more balanced sound profile than the bass-heavy Beats of the past. They also support Personalized Spatial Audio with dynamic head tracking, which creates an immersive, theater-like experience for movies and music. It is worth noting that these are "on-ear" headphones, meaning they rest directly on your ears rather than surrounding them. While the new UltraPlush cushions are incredibly soft, they prioritize a secure fit that stays put during movement, which some users may find a bit snug during very long listening sessions. title: Worth the price, good sound enough, perfect for workouts
body: I'm a huge headphone collector always looking for good quality sets. I have a lot of sets for particular purposes. Yes is right that these don't come with noise cancelling and that music doesn't stop when you take them off... but I was not looking for any high end/premium headphones, but instead something more simple, classic, that could deliver good sound quality. It is also true that its bass is not what it used to be, but this is to help to provide a better and more crisp sound. Apple indeed made a downgrade and I'm not a full fan of downgrading bass, but being able to use the spatial sound feature in these is great, it makes music much more enjoyable with clear vocals and you can actually distinguish instruments better than just having a full blast on the bass. Again, I wasn't going for a noise cancelling set, and its "passive" noise cancelling is good enough (not for very loud places like airplanes though) but that's why I have other sets in case I want noise cancelling. Love their size btw, small, compact enough to fit in a my already bulky backpack. It came with a very nice case/pack where I can fill with a few more stuff, so that's a big plus. Read more Read more

title: Perfect fit and sound quality restored
body: These replacement AirPod ear tips are excellent and honestly just as good as the originals. The fit is snug and secure, and they stay in place even during longer listening sessions or while moving around. Installation took seconds and felt intuitive — they snap on properly without feeling loose or fragile. Sound quality improved immediately after swapping out my old tips. Bass feels fuller, audio is clearer, and passive noise isolation is noticeably better, which makes a big difference for calls and music. Comfort is also a big plus — no pressure or irritation, even after extended use. The material feels high quality and durable, not cheap silicone, and they hold their shape well. I also appreciate that they match the original look of the AirPods, so once they’re on, you can’t really tell they’re replacements. Overall, this is a simple, affordable upgrade that restores both comfort and performance. If your original tips are worn out or lost, these are an easy five-star replacement and well worth it.

title: Great but they hurt
body: See Update at end, no more pain: Almost all reviews have no problem with the fit of these earbuds, but for me, they hurt. Not unbearably so, but it would be nice to not feel any discomfort at all. I tried changing the tips, adjusting the angle in my ear and also tried leaving loosely outside of ear canal, but always have noticeable discomfort. Plus if I leave them loose, I lose sound quality, so I don't like doing that. Would like to take away two stars but only removing one because these are really good earbuds otherwise for the price. I have never run out of power. Usually when I recharge the case, it doesn't even need recharging. I just do it to go back up to 100%. Despite the fact that I can feel them, it's not excruciating pain, so I do use them often. I just have to keep playing around with other tips and other means of making them more comfortable for me. The case is very attractive. In fact, someone saw my case with buds and commented, "Wow, those are nice!" I don't mind the size. I think it might be the reason the charge lasts so long. If you cut a deck of cards in half and placed on top of each other, that's about the size of the case. The earbuds themselves are attractive but also bulky. Maybe all earhook buds are bulky, I don' t know as these are my first. I wish they had skintone colors to make them less noticeable. I ordered rose gold thinking they would be less noticeable, but should've just gone with black. The sound quality, to me, is exceptional. I use for listening to music, to lessons, and have used for TV. I have not used for phone calls because I rarely use these thru my phone. Read more Read more
