product_name: JBL Vibe Beam 2 - True Wireless Noise Cancelling Earbuds with JBL Pure Bass Sound & Smart Ambient technology, 4mics for crisp, clear calls, up to 40Hrs of Playback, IP54 Dust & Water resistant (Black)
date: Reviewed in the United States on April 25, 2026
title: Pleasantly surprised :)
body: I got mine today and nothing is wrong with it, works exactly as described. I love that you can control almost everything about the sound, using the JBL headphones app. You can fully customize the equalizer settings to get the sound you like. Maybe I’m just used to using cheaper earbuds, but these are the best pair I’ve ever used. There is one feature in particular that I find extremely useful— The ambient and talk-through modes allow you to hear EVERYTHING around you, in real time, with no delay. It amplifies the sounds around you, including your own voice, without being annoying or disorienting. It balances those sounds with any media/music you play. Both can be heard clearly- you don’t need to take out an earbud to hear your surroundings, while still getting an immersive audio experience. On the other hand, the noise-cancelling function works better than other earbuds I’ve used. It is VERY quiet in this mode, perfect for when you DONT want to hear everything around you. It’s easy and quick to switch between these modes. You can use the app to switch it instead of touching the earbud itself, if you’d like. But out-of-the-box, it is a touch control function. There are many other features but the ones I’ve just described are my favorites. GET IT ON SALE THO DONT SPEND A FORTUNE ON THESE… I’ve seen other people saying it wasn’t worth that much. But I am happy after getting these for $39. Read more Read more

The replacement earbuds showed a vast improvement in mic quality, but it was evident that the earbuds were not new, and came with physical imperfections. I was not impressed. title: Not bad, but not a five star product.
body: I purchased it as a replacement for the LG TV remote that I had for 2 years. On first use it was very easy to set up. Instant connection from the time that I put the batteries in. Voice controls did not work. The function as a magic remote was not worth the extra dollars. We will have to see if it lives longer than its predecessor. There are no instructions on how to set up the multicolored other Functional buttons, and therefore are essentially useless. Overall, the instant connectivity and thus minimal setup make it an excellent replacement remote. I’m not sure that at double the cost of others that it is a worthwhile price for the nonfunctioning magic, remote and 4 of 10 nonfunctioning quick buttons.

product_name: Hanes mens Underwear Boxer Briefs Pack, Cool & Breathable Cotton Moisture-wicking Underwear for Men, No Ride Up
date: Reviewed in the United States on March 13, 2026
title: Good Bulk Buy, but Not for All-Day Sitting or Active Jobs
body: I originally bought this 12-pack for my husband back in 2023 because the bulk price was hard to beat. For the first few months, they were a great budget-friendly staple, but after nearly two years of use, we’ve seen where they start to fall short. Durability & Wearability: Initially, these were soft and comfortable for daily wear. However, after several months of regular washing, the black color began to fade significantly, and the fabric started to lose its "new" feel. Waistband & Sturdiness: The waistband starts out firm, but over time, it loses its elasticity. It doesn't have the long-term sturdiness needed to keep its shape, which leads to the dreaded "rollability" issue—the waistband eventually starts to curl and roll down during movement. Support & "The Bunch": My husband is a heavy equipment operator, so he spends his day sitting and moving in a cab. After the first few months, the support just wasn't there anymore. He found that the legs would roll up and the fabric would bunch up uncomfortably while he was working. Absorbency: They are 100% cotton, so they are naturally absorbent and breathable, which is a plus for moisture-wicking. However, once the fit becomes loose and "baggy," that benefit is outweighed by the lack of support. Read more Read more

product_name: Crocs Unisex-Adult Classic Clog, Clogs for Women and Men
date: Reviewed in the United States on February 28, 2026
title: Nice enough product, but don't order your first pair online
body: The shoes themselves are comfortable, durable (so far), easy to clean, everything that you'd expect from the description. I went from a Crocs-hater to an appreciater. But don't order online until you're familiar with the sizing. The manufacturer doesn't offer half-sizes, Amazon recommends ordering a size down if you're in-between, and supposedly the shoes will stretch a bit with use... but in practice that did not work for us. If you have wide feet, or any issues with foot/toe swelling, it definitely makes sense to try the shoes on in-person first. Even wearing socks can make a big difference, because the rubber material is high-friction and doesn't allow "slipping in" easily. I eventually ended up with two pairs in different sizes, rotated seasonally based on weather (socks) and foot health. If you have a local sporting-goods chain beginning with "D" and rhyming with "Kicks", buy your first pair there, where you can try them on (and support the local employees)... then use Amazon for repeat purchases (since Amazon is currently about $15 cheaper). 5 stars for the product, 3 stars for ordering online.

date: Reviewed in the United States on March 28, 2026
title: Best bra I’ve ever worn!
body: So, TMI. I am a 22 year breast cancer survivor that went through reconstructive surgery. After 22 years, the surgery has started to deteriorate. This means that I have one side that is a A cup and the other side is a C cup. It is almost impossible to find a bra or bathing suit that doesn’t make me look or feel like a freak. I do have a silicone inserts for the smaller cup size, but it is very uncomfortable and hot. This bra is absolutely amazing. Even without underwire it’s incredibly supportive. It does not roll up or ride up like many bras do without underwire. It fits perfectly and the bra straps are wide enough that they don’t keep slipping off of your shoulder. And best of all, I actually look normal when I wear a tighter shirt with it on. It is comfortable, soft, and frankly the best bra I have found so far. Even if you don’t have the challenges that I do finding a bra, I think that you would be very pleased with this. My only problem with it is it has three hooks in the back. I hate the three hooks! So I simply hook it on and loosen the straps and just put it over my head. The plastic piece to tighten the straps is actually towards the front so it’s very easy to adjust once you have it on. All in all it’s a perfect bra!

I've injured both shoulders and have a long upper torso, and many bras come with straps that aren't quite long enough for me, and that rest right over previously repaired parts of my shoulder, causing pain with minutes or hours from pressure. Good support is crucial, as the pull on upper chest muscles & ligaments from inadequately supported breasts also causes me discomfort & pain. I've tried every style & type of bra & strap out there - x back, convertibles, strapless, wired & wireless, front close, sports bras, you name the brand & stule, I've tried it. Plus all the "extras" - strap extenders & pads, tape & silicome "cutlets", etc. Nothing that felt even halfway tolerable worked and still looked good under clothing except heavily padded ports or wired bras with stiff, shaped cups that made my breasts look even larger, and reminded of women's magazine ads in the 1960's for hard, pointy-cup bras. And, when menopausal hot flashes or hot, humid weather hit, those few tolerable bras became soaked, leaving me with swamp boob, rashes & carrying a spare dry bra whenever I left home. Prepping for my 3rd shoulder surgery, I decided to search for a few inexpensive non-underwire bras with straps that could be unhooked or completely removed, for wearing while trussed up like a turkey for a few weeks post-op. Wired ones are quite uncomfortable and rub my underarm raw when my arm is strapped against my side & immobilized for 4-6 weeks, and I needed something supportive that wouldn't rub or pull on the operated areas, and to keep me "decent" during visits for PT & dr appts post op. I found these, read reviews, and gave them a try. I take a 38-42 DD, depending on brand & style, in Olga, Bali, Maidenform, Glamorise, etc., and 1 side, after 3 shoulder surgeries, is less full on top & sags a bit lower due to ligament damage, so it doesn't quite fill out a DD cup without help, and that shoulder slopes a bit. I usually need to play with straps & add a little lift in that cup so my chest looks even & that strap doesn't slide off. Going by the LetsJoli sizing chart, I decided to order the 2XL. date: Reviewed in the United States on April 5, 2026
title: Expensive Look Without The Expense!
body: Sizing: If you’re a true large like me and close to but less than fifteen pounds overweight, normal length and with a 44” chest, buy a medium and use the shirt to inspire you to lose the beer belly. If you have similar dimensions but are overweight by up to thirty pounds, get the large. Materials: The mediums are manufactured to 70% synthetic, 30% linen—which is a great mix, as is doesn’t have to be ironed after it’s washed and hung to dry, nor does it have to be ironed every time you get up from a chair. I’ve had many 100% linen shirts and sure, they look great after you spend thirty minutes ironing them, just to look like a crumpled up newspaper after you get out of your car. My experience with linen shirts is what brought me here. Unboxing the shirt: I threw it on an ironing board the moment I unbagged it. I tried the Synthetic setting on the iron—it wasn’t enough. I then tried the Wool setting and set the steam setting to constant medium to high. It worked great. I didn’t have to press down at all, just lightly glided the iron across wrinkled areas of the shirt. Within five minutes, the shirt looked like a hundred-dollar shirt. The material is light, yet feels heavy to the touch and breathes very well in the heat. The hidden buttons give a clean, modern look that allows the shirt to be worn with anything from shorts and sandals to dress slacks and shoes in a club. I like to wear these shirts, untucked with a pair of jeans and western boots. This shirt is very comfortable, fits nice and is extremely versatile. Read more Read more

body: Several months ago, I purchased some new cotton bikini underwear and prefer them over other, more revealing styles. date: Reviewed in the United States on April 12, 2026
title: good durable shirts
body: The Carhartt K87 pocket t-shirt is a classic for a reason. The heavyweight cotton material feels durable and high quality, making it great for both work and everyday wear. It holds up well over time and doesn’t feel flimsy like cheaper t-shirts. The loose fit is one of the standout features—it gives you plenty of room to move and stays comfortable even during long days. The chest pocket is a nice touch for small items, and the overall construction feels solid with minimal twisting after washing. Comfort-wise, it starts a bit stiff but softens up after a few washes, which is pretty typical for thicker cotton shirts. It’s also available in a wide range of colors, so it’s easy to grab a few and rotate them. The main downside is the fit can run large and slightly boxy, so you might want to size down depending on how you like your shirts to fit. Some people may also find the heavyweight fabric a bit warm in hot weather. Overall, it’s a reliable, long-lasting t-shirt that delivers great value for the price and is perfect if you want something durable and simple.

product_name: Nippies Nipple Covers for Women - Reusable Sticky Silicone Pasties with Travel Case - Original Circle or Lift Style Available
date: Reviewed in the United States on December 30, 2024
title: Effective and Comfortable (especially for smaller busts)
body: First off I feel very weird writing a review for this product BUT it’s been such a game changer for me that, despite feeling awkward, I wanted to write a review. I have a ridiculously small chest, I wear a 32AA but if the size existed I’d honestly fit better in a 28-30AA. Therefore I have an EXTREMELY hard time finding any real bra that fits me well and what does fit the best is sports bras. Unfortunately most sports bras don’t really have enough padding to conceal the outlines of this part of the breast (unfortunately with being a very small bust size I feel as though it just makes it even more noticeable) especially with any semi-tight top and there is nothing I hate more than having it show even slightly. For most of my life I’ve just avoided tops that are tighter but with a recent wardrobe change it became unavoidable and I needed some lighter browns which made the whole situation worse. So I decided to give this a try. I am so so so glad I did! It gives me enough coverage that even with a lightly padded sports bra I can still wear a more form fitting lighter colored top. They last all day (even in the height of summer and being outside in the sun all day and being fairly sweaty). I’ve reused the same pair multiple times and the adhesive despite numerous times hand washing. To ensure a good firm stick I do take a witch hazel toner over the area to remove any lotion, I’m not sure it is necessary to do that but I just want to make sure they stay put. I have very sensitive skin and despite the great grip it never bothers me! The only thing I kind of dislike is that if you sweat when you remove these there will be a dampness to the skin and covers BUT the dampness never ever leaks or shows and it never causes any kind of a rash or reaction on me. In fact the first time I used these was on a 90° day and after 6 hours outside and being hot dressed up in my Ren Faire clothes I was completely taken by surprise when I did remove them and discovered it was slightly damp, so you really can’t tell. I’m personally not a person that sweats much so my sweating in those conditions is much less than most people so perhaps someone else would have a different experience with these. The lack of sweating also means body odor really isn’t something I really worry about (I mean I use deodorant because you should not because I actually need to and that’s about the extent of my worry) but even with that dampness there was no odor. So for me that one things I’m not fond of is totally endurable because this product allows me to wear so many more tops with less self-consciousness! I have no idea if these are better or the same as cheaper brands because this was the first I tried and it worked so well I don’t plan on switching! I even swam with these once and they stayed on all day. I play volleyball, catch, frisbee, etc in the pool and don’t often dunk and swim a ton so if you swam submerged for hours I have no idea if they’d stay but for my activity that includes some dunking these things didn’t budge! I only got to try them once but this is going to allow me to wear a lot of bikini tops I would have avoided before! Seriously, life changing! Read more Read more

date: Reviewed in the United States on March 28, 2011
title: UPDATED 4/2/12: One of the best purchases I've ever made.
body: I'm a small B-cup and don't really need a bra, and find bras uncomfortable. So, I bought my first pair of adhesive Nippies back in January. I have worn them nearly every day since then, and for me, they have been about as perfect as a product could be. Comfort: They're extremely comfortable -- so comfortable, in fact, that I occasionally forget to take them off before going to sleep because I forget they're there. I was afraid that the adhesive might irritate my skin, but it hasn't at all. They stay on very well and are easy to wash after use (I use plain old soap and water). Coverage: The coverage they provide is remarkably good, given how extremely thin they are. I can wear a form-fitting white tee-shirt with just the Nippies underneath, and my nipples aren't visible. I recently tried one other brand of nipple covers (Spicy Spot) because they were less expensive, and although they were thicker than Nippies, they were almost completely transparent and my nipples were quite visible under light-colored shirts. So in terms of how well they do their job, Nippies have definitely been worth the money for me. I love not having to worry about bra straps showing! Lifespan: As I said, I wore my first pair almost every day for 3+ months, so my estimate is that they've lasted about 100 wears. They have just now gotten to the point where enough of the adhesive has worn away that I can't wear them with looser shirts (though they're still fine with more form-fitting shirts, which hold them in place). date: Reviewed in the United States on June 7, 2011
title: great product but read the fine print
body: I got these to try out because I recently purchased some new bras that are not padded; just thinly lined. I had hoped with this horrid summer heat and humidity the unpadded bras would not get so damp from me sweating. The problem with the unlined bras is nipple show through so, after reading about various products in this category, I tried these. They are simple to apply and come in a rather sturdy flip-top case that can be used to keep them clean and in shape. Upon arrival they have tissue-like paper against the adhesive part and each is set on top of a thin plastic domed form for keeping them separated and in proper shape. To apply, I peeled away the tissue paper, placed the product adhesive side up in the center of my palm, leaned over and applied it, holding it in place a second or two as instructed so body heat helps adhere the adhesive to skin. It was smooth and flat without any wrinkles or puckers. That was at 4am this morning when I got dressed. It was pretty much invisible and I must admit, once I put my bra in place I could not tell I was wearing them. I could tell, however, that I had no peek-through or poke-through of my bra or my thin summer tank top. That is until I stopped in the grocery store and got to the frig/freezer sections. By the time I got out of that section of the grocery store, nothing short of a patio brick would have stopped my show through so if that is the reason you're looking at these, I'm not sure they will be the cover you desire. date: Reviewed in the United States on April 1, 2026
title: Great fit and comfort at an affordable price
body: After losing a significant amount of weight, I found it impossible to find a bra that fit. I bought many bras that have ended up un-worn in the back of a drawer. I am happy to have finally found a bra that fits and is comfortable. I ordered size small. The material and banding feel great. All parts of the bra stay in place. Nothing shifts around and the straps don't fall down. Snap extension are provided, if needed. The wide bands help smooth the underarm and back area. These bras are lightly padded, and I do not like bras that don't have the padding completely stitched down all the way around because the padding bunches up in the wash and is difficult or impossible to straighten out. The only drawback to these bras is that the padding isn't stitched all the way around. After one hand-washing (as recommended), the padding stayed in place. Hand-washing is inconvenient so I may try to machine wash one of the 3 I bought to see if the padding remains in place. At any rate, if I have to completely hand-stitch the padding in myself, it will be worth it for the comfort and sizing these bras provide. The pricing is good for the quality of these bras. I will definitely be buying more. I wish they came in a dark brown color.

date: Reviewed in the United States on April 24, 2026
title: Small band/large breasted women lose again (too big and too small at the same time)
body: So bummed. This bra was super comfy, BUT … the band was too big, and the cups too small. I was on the last clasp available, and still wished there was another row or two to further tighten it. But at the same time, I was spilling out of the tops of the cup. If I rearranged I could get them “un-spilled” at the top, there was spillage in the underarm area (women, you know what I mean by this…). Granted, I’m a busty gal. I wear a 36 H, sometimes a G, and sometimes an I … it depends on the bra. But I read everything I could on this bra - and read a lot of reviews too - so I thought I was making a good choice with my size, but … nope. I considered trying a replacement by going up a size, but if the cups fit on a larger one, surely, the band would be even bigger as well … and I was already wishing for a tighter band. So, return it I must. Bummer drag. I loved the feel of it, and it DID provide lift; I could also see that it would have given a nice shape if only there had been a teeny bit more room in the cups. The material was buttery and soft and I really liked it; I’m sad to have to return it. We small backed but large breasted women lose again. 😕

title: A RIP OFF when trying to refund/return, SIZING WAY WAY OFF
body: Sizing is Way, way off. I ordered according to their chart, the band was at least 6 inches apart from where it should hook up, the shoulder straps were so short the bra cups were around my neck. When I requested a refund, the return label was written in Chinese with a Chinese address, contrary to Amazon's return policy: Third-Party Seller Returns When you order from a third-party seller that fulfills and ships their own inventory, your return is sent back to the seller instead of Amazon. Sellers must offer one of the following: A return address within Canada where you can ship the item A prepaid return label; or A full refund without requesting the item be returned For more information about returns to sellers, please refer to Returns to Third-Party Sellers. And, according to other purchasers, the cost to return was more than the cost of the item. So, basically a rip off.

product_name: Comfort Colors Adult Heavyweight Short Sleeve Tee, Style G1717/G1745, Multipack
date: Reviewed in the United States on October 5, 2025
title: Comfort Colors T-shirt sizing is inconsistent from shirt to shirt
body: Do a search in the reviews of these T-shirts using the word “inconsistent” and you’ll see others have similar comments to mine. With almost 27,000 reviews on these shirts, not sure if anyone will even see this review, but here we go. I have about 5 Comfort Colors T-shirts that I got in the past year; all are size Large, and they fit great in the body and neck with nice soft cotton, and they are fantastic T-shirts. I recently ruined a denim-colored Comfort Colors T-shirt using Spray-n-Wash on a couple food stains on the chest; the Spray-n-Wash faded the blue color in the treated areas. No worries, I’ll just order a replacement shirt…. I decided to order two replacement Comfort Colors T-shirts with one being blue jean-colored and the second being a denim-colored T-shirt. Both are blue with blue jean being slightly lighter blue than the denim, but I decided to order two since I like these T-shirts so much. The blue jean shirt arrived and fit great and is just like all my other large Comfort Colors shirts, and it’s a keeper. However, the denim shirt I received was too small: it was tight in the chest, the body was shorter, and the sleeves were shorter. I have attached two photos with one showing this new denim shirt on top of the shirt I ruined with Spray-n-Wash and another showing this new denim shirt on top of an orange Custom Colors shirt to show the smaller size of the new shirt. The neck-size in the new denim shirt I received was fine, but the shirt was noticeably too small in all other dimensions. I immediately ordered another denim-colored shirt hoping to get a “correct size” shirt, and the second denim shirt arrived the next day, and it was also smaller in all the dimensions exactly like the first denim shirt I had just ordered. All three of these new Comfort Colors shirts (one blue jean and two denim) and the denim shirt I ruined with Spray-n-Wash were sold and delivered by Amazon (i.e. none were third party vendors). I’m returning both of the too-small denim-colored shirts, and I’ll live without that color and enjoy my new blue jean shirt. Read more Read more

product_name: Rustler Men's Classic Relaxed Fit
date: Reviewed in the United States on March 18, 2026
title: Why I only buy Rustler, Wrangler and Lee.
body: Why I only buy Rustler and Lee (Protect your pride and your comfort) If you want to know why most modern jeans feel like trash, it’s the fabric. Take a look at Gap, Levi’s, and the other big "fashion" brands. Most of them aren't even 100% cotton anymore—they’re full of plastic and "stretch" fibers. Real men don't need plastic in their pants. Those cheap brands also use a Right-Hand Twill weave (running bottom-left to top-right). Over time, that weave forces the jeans to twist, making the left leg tighter and mushing your GONADS into your leg. It’s annoying, it’s uncomfortable, and it’s weak. If you value your testosterone and your comfort, stick to Lee, Rustler, and Wrangler. Rustler and Wrangler use a Broken Twill weave. It’s a heavy-duty, 15oz, 100% cotton denim that actually molds to your body instead of fighting it. Because the weave is balanced, it won't twist and crush your anatomy. Lee uses a high-quality Left-Hand Twill that stays soft and straight without that cheap shrinking and leg-twist you get from the other guys. Do your body and Western Civilization a favor: make peace with your GONADS, be a man, and buy a pair of heavy-duty Rustlers. High-quality jeans for a price that doesn't break the bank. product_name: Wrangler Authentics Men's Classic Cargo Stretch Short
date: Reviewed in the United States on April 23, 2026
title: Comfortable and convenient
body: Got these to replace some old cargo shorts that aren't made anymore and are developing holes. So far I have been very pleased. The waist fits true to size and the fit is relaxed through the seat and thigh, which is exactly what I was looking for since I have somewhat stout thighs relative to my waist. I'm 6'0", about 200lbs, with a 34-35" waist. The material feels sturdy without being overly heavy, about as thick as old ripstop BDUs. Front slash pockets are generously deep; no problem carrying a 5.5" smartphone in there. Rear pockets are sufficiently deep and are narrow enough that a trifold wallet won't turn itself sideways. Cargo pockets are adequately sized and have hook and loop closures at the corners. The "tech" pocket behind the left cargo pocket is very convenient for me, as I'm used to carrying my phone on that side. I can slide my Samsung Galaxy S25 in there and it fits well without worrying about it falling out. I wore these for a couple of days at Disney World last week and they performed very well. Moving my phone to that tech pocket made rides with lap bars or belts much more comfortable. I even washed these shorts at the resort so I could wear them on the long drive home. If I could change anything about them, I'd add about an inch of length, as I prefer a full 11" inseam on cargo shorts. And maybe add a button or some kind of closure to the rear pockets. But aside from that, these have been pretty close to perfect. I expect I'll order more as I replace more of my older cargo shorts. Read more Read more

date: Reviewed in the United States on March 26, 2026
title: runs big
body: I am always a size 8 in boots, sandals, sneakers and read these ran large so ordered a size 7.5. They are still too big on me. I sent back for a 7 and I will update when I get those. They are very, very cute and comfortable! I love the cloth that goes over the foot!! That makes them seem a bit more fancy than regular flip flops. They also do sound like regular flip flops, so if you want a quiet sandal these are not for you. They are very cushioned and I can def feel the arch support. You will need to size down at least half size and maybe one whole size. The hardest thing for me was to pick a color! May have to get several pair! Just ordered my mom the dark blue and I know she will love them!!! Highly recommend, especially for the low price! Update: Got the 7 and are still too big. As I said, I got my mom the 6 so I tried them on and they are still a tad too big on me!!! BTW the sizes 8,7.5,7 and the 6 I can hardly see any difference whatsoever! They don't come in 5 1/2 so I am sending the 7 back for a 6 and keeping those just because they are so darn cute and comfortable! I also don't have a very thick foot so I wish the straps were a tad tighter, but I will deal with it. So, to sum up: Be sure to size down at least one size and maybe even two. Returns are easy, so maybe try a few different sizes! I haven't worn a size 6 since I was in about the 6th grade, so not sure what is going on with the sizing. Oh yeah, I did get three different colors and the sizing was the same with all three. HTH! Good luck! Read more Read more

date: Reviewed in the United States on April 7, 2026
title: Nice simple flip-flops, but the straps are soft fabric that collapse
body: Nice and simple flip-flop. I'll wear these around the house, the yard, and outside. They're what I call "multi-tasking" flip-flops. But, keep in mind, the straps are a soft fabric.....at least the ones I got in WHITE.......and that means they collapse and lay down on the flip-flop, so it's not easy to just slide into these and go. You have to hold up the straps & move them around, in order to wear these. I've had many, many flip-flops in my time, and the straps are always structured & standing up. Slide into them, and go. Not with these. That's the only negative feature. They're comfortable, true-to-size, and the strap doesn't hurt me at all between my toes (as some others have said it hurts them). All-in-all, I'm happy with this item and will keep it, but get ready to adjust & fiddle with the strap before you can start walking.

date: Reviewed in the United States on March 2, 2026
title: Accurate, Compact, and Great for Avoiding Airline Surprises
body: This luggage scale is a simple tool that solves a real travel problem. It’s compact enough to keep in a carry-on or suitcase pocket, and it’s easy to use—clip it to the handle, lift briefly, and get a clear weight reading. The display is easy to read, and the measurements have been consistent when compared against airline scales. The strap feels secure when lifting heavier checked bags, and the overall design is lightweight without feeling flimsy. It’s especially helpful when packing close to airline weight limits, since it removes the guesswork and helps avoid overweight baggage fees at the airport. The reason this isn’t a full five-star rating is that the plastic body, while functional, feels cheap rather than premium. It works exactly as intended, but it doesn’t have a high-end build feel. For practical travel use and peace of mind at a reasonable price, it’s a very useful addition to any travel kit.

date: Reviewed in the United States on March 29, 2026
title: Great luggage scale!
body: This Travel Inspira luggage scale is a total lifesaver for anyone who travels regularly. It’s compact, lightweight, and incredibly easy to use—perfect for throwing into your bag without taking up any space. I never have to stress about overweight baggage fees anymore, which alone makes it worth it. The accuracy is spot-on, and the 110 lb (50 kg) capacity is more than enough for even larger suitcases. I also really appreciate the clear backlit LCD display—it’s easy to read in any lighting, whether you’re in a dim hotel room or a bright airport. The data lock feature is super helpful too, giving you time to check the weight without it disappearing instantly. Comfort-wise, the rubberized handle makes it easy to grip, even when lifting heavier bags. It feels sturdy and well-made, not flimsy like some other portable scales. The auto-off function is a nice bonus that helps preserve battery life, and it’s great that the battery is included right out of the box. Overall, this is a simple but essential travel tool that delivers exactly what it promises. Reliable, convenient, and stress-saving—I wouldn’t travel without it now. Highly recommended!

date: Reviewed in the United States on November 26, 2025
title: Reliable fashion tape
body: Removing the tape can be a little uncomfortable — it felt a bit like pulling off a band-aid (though the discomfort was brief). Because the hold is strong, if your skin is sensitive or you have a lot of movement (dancing, brisk walking, etc.), I’d recommend doing a small patch test first — on a less visible area — to make sure you’re okay with the adhesive. For some very delicate fabrics (like thin silk or lace), you might need to be cautious — I avoided heavy pulling or repositioning once the tape was on. If you want a simple, effective fashion solution for dresses, low-cut tops, strapless outfits, or just to prevent wardrobe malfunctions, this tape is worth owning. It’s not perfect — the removal isn’t completely painless — but the convenience and peace of mind it offers make it a solid 4-star product in my book. I’d recommend it to anyone who wants reliable “stay-in-place” support without bulky undergarments.

date: Reviewed in the United States on January 12, 2026
title: Great hold, not not without consequences
body: I really wanted to give this product 5 stars because the hold is incredible, unfortunately, the after effects force me to give it 3 stars. I’ve used this tape twice, both times with the same outfit: the “By the Lake” Padmé Amidala dress. The first time was on May the 4th at Disney, worn for about 5 hours. It held perfectly, even in the Florida heat, and I had zero issues. The second time, I wore it for nearly 12 hours. Once again, the hold itself was flawless, nothing shifted or moved. However, after several hours I began feeling pain and irritation where the tape was applied. When it came time to remove the dress, I had to be extremely careful because of how strongly the tape adhered to my skin. Despite being cautious, the tape peeled off skin in multiple areas, and I later developed very painful warts in those same spots. This was especially surprising because I do not have sensitive skin. My takeaway: this tape works very well, but I’d urge people to use it with caution. Avoid wearing it for long periods, and be especially careful if you have sensitive skin. If you do wear it for an extended time, be prepared for possible skin damage when removing it. Read more Read more

product_name: Hanes Men's Beefy-t T-Shirt, Heavyweight Cotton Tee, 1 Or 2 Pack, Big & Tall
date: Reviewed in the United States on October 26, 2025
title: Good Quality But Sleeves Are An Issue
body: I'm giving this shirt 4 stars for one reason: the sleeves. I'm not sure why big and tall sizes have sleeve seams that go down so far past the shoulder and have a length that goes past the elbow. It's like you're wearing a tent. Why not make the chest and belly area bigger, which they do, but make the sleeves more proportionate? Not everyone who wears a 4XL is elephant-sized with long gorilla arms. I'd wager most aren't. With that said, the quality of the t-shirt is nice, and it's comfortable. It's a thicker cotton that held up well after a wash and didn't shrink too much. It fits well and is longer than a typical 4XL. I haven't noticed any loose threads or discoloration. It's well-made, and, given the price, is hard to complain about, despite my aforementioned gripe. I'm using these as blanks to press my own designs, so even if the sleeves are aggravating, it's cheaper to make your own clothes to wear than to spend $40 for a single graphic t-shirt at a big and tall store. If you can look past the sleeve issue, these t-shirts might be a good fit for you.

date: Reviewed in the United States on November 6, 2025
title: Great, quality shirt I ordered 2 more 2-packs
body: I recently purchased the Hanes Men's Long-Sleeve Beefy Henley Shirt in XL, and I'm thoroughly impressed with its quality and design. In an era where current fashion trends push brands to make shirts as thin and cheap as possible—often using flimsy fabrics that pill after one wash—this Henley stands out as a refreshing throwback to durable, substantial clothing. The thickness of the material is one of my favorite features; it's hefty enough to feel premium and provide real warmth without being overly bulky, making it perfect for layering or wearing solo in cooler weather.The long-sleeve style is another highlight—classic and versatile, with a three-button placket that adds a touch of casual elegance. Whether I'm heading to a casual office day, running errands, or lounging at home, it fits seamlessly into my wardrobe. The cotton blend is soft against the skin yet tough enough to withstand repeated wear and laundering without losing shape or fading. I've worn it through several cycles already, and it still looks brand new.One note on sizing: these run a little larger than expected. I opted for an XL based on my usual fit, but an L would have worked just as well for a more tailored look without sacrificing comfort. If you're between sizes, definitely consider sizing down for the best result.Overall, this shirt exceeds expectations in build, style, and value. It's a reliable staple that proves you don't have to compromise on quality to stay stylish. Highly recommend for anyone tired of disposable tees—grab one (or a few) and feel the difference! body: This tank top is very comfortable and easy to wear, especially because of the built‑in bra. product_name: CRZ YOGA Butterluxe Yoga Leggings 25" - High Waisted Buttery Soft Womens Workout Lounge Pants
date: Reviewed in the United States on October 9, 2025
title: Amazing Leggings! But when buying leggings for practice, you get what you pay for :)
body: First of all, let me say, these leggings are AMAZING: they're comfortable, fit perfectly, the material is as described - literally buttery - and they look incredible. This color is also gorgeous, it took a while for my size to restock just to get this color and it was worth the wait. HOWEVER, these being my first pair or CRZ leggings, I am a bit disappointed. I practiced in them 3 times and they seem really solid for moderate to low impact practice, especially Hot. They're very breathable. (I'm talking yoga here, but they'd also be very good for gym or running). Here's the CATCH: I wore them last weekend under a pair of sweats while hiking. I'm not sure how or when it happened, but after I got home, I noticed a huge hole just below the butt in the back center of my right leg. It looked a lot like a nylon tear or rip, rather than a tiny hole that you'd see with more durable, expensive fabrics. I say all this to say that when it comes to clothing whose durability you rely on, you really get what you pay for. I.E. My first pair of Lulu leggings were a pair of black Aligns and I wore them 2 years straight, never once ripping, snagging, no holes or otherwise, and I just regifted them because they still look nearly brand new. I am glad to know how CRZ's hold up now so I can use them for less rugged activity. Regardless, I still love them and I will still be purchasing this brand. I also won't be throwing them out, I've worn them a total of 4x, so I WILL find a way to layer or repair. Read more Read more

product_name: CRZ YOGA Butterluxe Yoga Leggings 25" - High Waisted Buttery Soft Womens Workout Lounge Pants
date: Reviewed in the United States on February 9, 2026
title: Good product!
body: Honest review with pros and cons. I'd give these a 4.5 Pros- no sagging, smooth-soft fabric, thin like the Align but with a slightly less natural feel. I got the oasis blue and it was a perfect match to advertisement photos. Sizing is as anticipated Cons- while the leggings do not lose shape or sag, the top does roll down. But its not obnoxious- for me anyway, and probably wont roll while doing standing exercises like jogging. As stated earlier, these have a slightly less natural feel than the Aligns- less soft, more slippery, but not fake feeling and similar thinness and breathability. Overall, i need a red pair of leggings and likely WILL purchase them from CRZ. My favorites for a similar feel: Old Navy's Studiosmooth and cloudcomfy are fantastic options. Lulu Align's take second place, due to their high cost. These CRZs are a solid 3rd place, and a good dupe overall. Read more Read more

title: The Most Comfortable Leggings I've Ever Worn!
body: CRZ YOGA Butterluxe Leggings have absolutely revolutionized my workout and loungewear wardrobe, delivering the perfect combination of comfort, performance, and style that I've been searching for! As someone who lives in leggings for everything from yoga classes to grocery runs, these have become my go-to choice that outperforms much more expensive alternatives. The buttery soft fabric is genuinely luxurious against the skin and feels like wearing a gentle hug all day long. The texture is smooth and silky without being slippery, creating incredible comfort that makes me forget I'm wearing them. Unlike other "soft" leggings that feel nice initially but become uncomfortable during extended wear, these maintain their comfort throughout entire days. What impressed me most is how the high-waisted design provides perfect coverage and support without creating the dreaded roll-down effect that plagues so many leggings. The waistband stays exactly where positioned throughout workouts, stretching, and daily activities while providing gentle compression that's supportive rather than restrictive. The 25-inch length is absolutely perfect for my 5'6" frame, hitting exactly at the ankle without being too long or too short. The proportions are flattering and versatile enough to work beautifully with sneakers, boots, or barefoot for yoga practice. The length creates a sleek, elongated silhouette that works for both athletic and casual styling. Read more Read more

product_name: CRZ YOGA Butterluxe Yoga Leggings 25" - High Waisted Buttery Soft Womens Workout Lounge Pants
date: Reviewed in the United States on December 16, 2025
title: Great for big tummies
body: A friend recommended these and I’m obsessed now. Just ordered another pair after only having these for a week. They’re so silky and smooth. No idea what pants size I am right now (16-20?) but I’m 195 pounds, big tummy (I look like I could be pregnant), big thighs, big calves, and big muffin top. The amount of space my stomach takes up when I’m sitting down vs standing is really different, so I’ve only been doing stretchy waistband clothes the last few years since trousers and jeans either dig in when I sit down or fall down when I’m walking. These are perfect. I only do high waisted and these are very high waisted. They don’t feel too tight at all, yet they do feel like they hug and support me. The top part has only folded a tiny bit once, when I had just gotten in the car. But around the house they DONT slide down at all, and don’t normally fold on me. They’re not hard to get on and off. I wear thongs since the seam of normal underwear can be seen, and unless I do a laser cut or “no seam” thong, I can see the faint outline of the top still, so I personally don’t wear these leggings with very cropped things. I also do darker colors because I am a little self conscious about how lighter colors don’t hide my extra “contours” with the shine of leggings as much as the darker colors, but I feel actually sexy and athletic in these. Just wearing these around the house gives me a confidence boost, and also a boost of encouragement to go to the gym. I’m not self conscious of my booty but my booty does look extra great in this lol. body: We were traveling on Thursday, April 9 and flew out of Charlotte, NC. I filled my Owala with cold tap water at home and drove 2.5 hours to Charlotte Airport. I took a sip from my Owala and it was still cold! Not as cold as when we left but cold! Your ad says “stays cold or hot for 25 hours!” You can change that to 120 hours! I love the features of the Owala. The colors are so fun, the straw feature to sip from is awesome or the spout is convenient as well. I love the carry handle and the push button release is excellent! title: Very nice color and bottle
body: The owala is truly wonderful and useful. Contrary to popular belief the Stanley still ties this with 1st place in the “water bottle race”. Seriously I think the Stanley is good for people who are in office jobs or who are staying in one place a lot and the owala is for people who move around at their job and need a water bottle with a top that is secure. An athlete might want an owala to throw in their bag and run with so that they can have the peace of mind knowing that it’s not spilling in their bag. A secretary at a school might want the Stanley because it looks very modern and sharp when sitting on an office desk, and also it has a straw that you can drink out of at work when your thirsty. I personally have both the Stanley and the owala, and I use the both interchangeably whenever I need each one. If I’m taking a trip I take the owala, if I’m sitting at home or in an office I use the Stanley.

product_name: bella 2 Slice Slim Toaster, Fits-anywhere™ Kitchenware, 6 Setting Shade Control with Reheat & Cancel Buttons, Fits sourdough, 10” Long Slot, Anti Jam & Auto Shutoff, 900 Watt, Oatmilk
date: Reviewed in the United States on March 17, 2026
title: Great price, works well, looks good
body: After about 20 years, our big-brand toaster broker. Most of the big-brand models cost over $100, and many over $200. That felt ridiculous for a device that basically just heats up coils. My spouse and I decided to try the Bella and it's great. At the time of this review, it cost $35. We got the 4 slice Oatmeal colored one. Looks great in our kitchen and pretty much works just like our old one. A few notes for those that are interested: - Has no bell to indicate it's done. That didn't bother us - especially if the other person is sleeping. - No digital display. The temp control is an old school knob that articulates very nicely. It took us a moment to get used to this because from a distance with our old toaster, we could see the temp setting and make corrections if we forgot to set it how we like it. Easy adjustment for us to make. - It has a removable crumb tray. This is important to us because we beat our toasters up by putting all kinds of foods into it which sends crumbles to the bottom all the time. - It doesn't have a "lift to look" feature...we kind of miss that but it's an easy adjustment for us to make. - Looks great - we do like having a little style / design in our kitchen. It looks like an up-scale product. Read more Read more

date: Reviewed in the United States on April 13, 2026
title: erfect Slim Toaster for RV Life – Fits Anywhere and Works Great
body: erfect Slim Toaster for RV Life – Fits Anywhere and Works Great This Bella 2-slice slim toaster is an absolute perfect fit for RV use and earns every bit of 5 stars. The slim, fits-anywhere design is exactly what you need in an RV where counter space is at a premium—it tucks away easily without taking up valuable workspace, and the oatmilk finish looks clean and modern in the kitchen area. The 10-inch long slot handles a variety of bread sizes without any issues, and the 6 shade settings give enough control to get toast exactly the way you like it every time. The reheat and cancel buttons are convenient everyday touches, and the auto shutoff gives peace of mind in a small RV kitchen where you don't want to leave anything unattended. The removable crumb tray makes cleanup quick and easy—an important feature in an RV where keeping things tidy in a small space matters. At 900 watts it toasts efficiently without being a heavy draw on the power system, which is a practical consideration for RV electrical loads. If you're looking for a compact, reliable, great-looking toaster that fits perfectly in an RV kitchen without compromise, this Bella slim toaster is exactly the right choice. Highly recommended.

product_name: bella 2 Slice Slim Toaster, Fits-anywhere™ Kitchenware, 6 Setting Shade Control with Reheat & Cancel Buttons, Fits sourdough, 10” Long Slot, Anti Jam & Auto Shutoff, 900 Watt, Oatmilk
date: Reviewed in the United States on April 28, 2026
title: Sleek design, works great, and perfect for small spaces
body: I really like this toaster — the slim design is honestly one of its best features. It doesn’t take up much counter space at all, which is perfect if you have a smaller kitchen or just want a cleaner setup. Performance-wise, it does exactly what you’d want. The 6 shade settings actually make a noticeable difference, so you can get your toast just how you like it. It toasts evenly on both sides, and I haven’t had any issues with uneven browning or needing to run it twice. The reheat function is also a nice bonus and works well for warming things back up without over-toasting them. It feels solid overall and not cheap, and the controls are simple and easy to use. If I had to point out one small thing, it’s that like most slim toasters, it’s better suited for standard bread slices rather than really thick artisan bread — but that’s expected with this style. Overall, great toaster for the price and especially ideal if you’re short on space but still want something reliable and good-looking.

date: Reviewed in the United States on August 31, 2025
title: Great for Cold & Iced Liquids
body: 4 stars — great for cold and especially iced liquids; poor for hot. No spillage at all. Would buy again. I recently tested a variety of tumblers with a digital thermometer, in a variety of conditions. Two important findings: (1) The best predictor of temperature after 3 or 24 hours was the tumbler's volume, not the brand or design — if you can stuff it with more ice, it will stay colder, longer. (2) The differences were too small to matter, in my opinion. After 3 hours, there was barely a 1-2°F among the tumblers. Even after 24 hours, the spread was under 6°F. Each retained Ice & Heat for long enough periods that I could prepare the night before. Accordingly, I chose based on design, ease of cleaning, and flavor. I was surprised that the Owala ended up the best for sipping cold liquids. Despite having a tiny opening to drink from, it never gets blocked by ice (crushed, cubed, or otherwise). It also hasn't gotten blocked by boba or passion fruit seeds or anything else I've had in the cup. Read more Read more

product_name: Fullstar The Original Pro Chopper - Vegetable Chopper and Spiralizer, Food Chopper with Container, Fruit Slicer, Kitchen Gadgets & Gifts, Home Essentials (4 in 1, White)
date: Reviewed in the United States on February 20, 2026
title: 3 years later, still chopping like new!
body: I bought this in May of 2023 and as of February of 2026, this little chopper is still going strong! It's super easy to use, makes chopping anything super fast, and the blades have stayed sharp as anything. I only use the two square blades, so I can't speak on the others, but if they are of the same quality, then I assume they would be great too. It cleans super well in the dishwasher as long as you brush out any bits of food in the top grid and on the blades first. Otherwise they might not wash off and end up dried to the blades, making it much harder to clean. I've had to clean the dried bits off more than once when my kid loaded the dishwasher and didn't make sure there were no food chunks on it. I especially love this thing for chopping onions. Thanks to the pieces being contained in the bin after being chopped, they don't have the opportunity to make my eyes water. I leave it all in the bin until it's time to dump it in the pan. I have recommended this thing to everyone I know, plus in posts on Reddit. It's probably my most used kitchen gadget, getting used almost every single time I cook. Read more Read more

product_name: Fullstar The Original Pro Chopper - Vegetable Chopper and Spiralizer, Food Chopper with Container, Fruit Slicer, Kitchen Gadgets & Gifts, Home Essentials (4 in 1, White)
date: Reviewed in the United States on March 29, 2026
title: Great for Certain Veggies but the Cleanup is a Chore
body: I purchased this vegetable chopper in mid-2023 to make meal prep more efficient and slightly more fun, but after over a year, my feelings are mixed. The assembly is straightforward as the different blade options snap and lock into the base easily, and the overall fit of the components is secure. It has a clean, professional appearance on the counter, but the actual functionality depends entirely on what you are trying to chop. While the machine offers versatility with several blade options, it doesn't meet the bar for everything in my kitchen. Soft vegetables like tomatoes are not good candidates as they tend to get smashed, and very hard vegetables like carrots are too difficult to push through. For me, it really only works well for bell peppers and onions, and even then, you have to cut the vegetables down significantly first unless you want to throw your entire body weight into it. The biggest drawback for me is the cleanup. The blades are not the easiest to clean, and even using the provided plastic claw, removing debris is rarely effortless. Since I only regularly use it for two types of vegetables and then freeze them in batches, I don't feel it provides great value for the money for my specific household. However, I can see where someone who chops a much wider variety of moderate-density produce daily would get more use out of this. Read more Read more

date: Reviewed in the United States on February 19, 2026
title: Acceptable for the Price
body: It seems absurd to spend $50, or even $100, on a meat thermometer. But if you want a good thermometer that works quickly and accurately, then the price is just a bullet you'll have to bite. I tested four meat thermometers: this one, the Thermopro Lightning ($45), the Thermopro TP620 ($30), the Thermopro TP19H ($15), and the Thermoworks ThermoPop 2 ($47). Some meat thermometers have some lag between the first displayed reading and the final accurate reading. With the TP19H, this lag is the most pronounced, as it takes between 10 and 20 seconds in many cases to reach an accurate reading. The TP620 and the ThermoPop 2, have less lag, but still take between 5 and 10 seconds to reach an accurate reading. The Thermopro Lightning, however, is a true instant-read thermometer, as it provides a fast initial reading, and the final accurate reading takes no more than two seconds to appear. The Lightning is the only one that I know that I can trust to quickly provide an accurate reading. I find this to be surprising given Thermoworks' reputation, but perhaps their Thermapen ONE, which I did not retest since it retails for $125, is what merits their reputation. For $45, however, the Thermoprop Lightning appears to be mightly close in performance to the much more expensive Thermoworks and is certainly better than the ThermoPop 2 that I tested. The ThermoPop doesn't even have a magnet or hook. All of these thermometers are waterproof under running water. I probably wouldn't put them in the dishwasher, however. The displays on all of these are fine, and I think that the Thermopro Lightning's display stands out quite well. If you're looking for a quality meat thermometer that will do exactly what you expect it to do, get the Lightning. But if you need the cheapest meat thermometer out there, the TP19H here will suffice. But be warned: the lag of the thermometer in providing an accurate reading will likely be frustrating and uncomfortable, especially over substantial heat, which is obviously the circumstance in which you're likely to be using this product. I've used all of these products, and I didn't want to spend $45 on a meat thermometer. At $45, the Thermopro Lightning isn't cheap, but if you can swing it, you'll have a for better meat thermometer that truly provides three times the value compared to this cheaper model. date: Reviewed in the United States on April 9, 2026
title: Well made, easy to clean, and no leaks.
body: It’s very good. Keeps ice for at least 18 hours. Stays super cold, doesn’t leak, easy to clean. Works great for hot liquids too but I would be worried about the straw as I become more aware of microplastics and begin to eliminate them from my life they are literally everywhere if you actually analyze what they are in and how the break down over time we have a really tough uphill climb. I would like to see this straw replaced with something that will never break down and get into our system period. We would ideally have to use only a product with steel insulated walls and no plastic contact, not even the o-ring. Even those break down and get into our liquids over time and that amount of time is very short. It doesn’t fit in all cup holders because of the cup holder depth, if it’s a deep cup holder then the handle gets in the way. Its versatile to an extent I guess, but it becomes cumbersome quickly depending on the application. I think it’s good for hangout at the pool or beach but beyond that its size and shape start to annoy pretty quickly. It’s also heavy if you’re loading with ice and liquid and if you add chopped fruit or veggies then you’ve got yourself a pretty weighty jug rapidly. Its also very tall because they try and accommodate standard cup holders so keep that in mind, it’s easy to knock over and if it’s full of ice, liquid and chopped fruit and veggies for flavor enhancing it’s quickly like carrying a small carryon or cinder block with a handle. Overall though it’s a tank, keeps liquids cold as heck, doesn’t leak, sturdy, and easy to clean but quickly can feel like you’re carrying a 5lb infant around, that’s top heavy, not stable in many standard cupholders becoming a cumbersome dumbbell quickly. Speaking of dumbbells it can be used as one in a pinch Read more Read more

body: I always stick with yeti and rtic but I needed something that fully closed and still had my beloved straw. Yeti/Rtic just didnt make something in any size that meets these requirements. I have arthritis and limited hand function at times and I find even with water in it I can hold it fine and it's not too heavy. I think my only 2 complaints are related to the lid. Its difficult to screw on and off (hopefully that will ease up the more I use it) and its not as thick and rigid like other lids ' the edges flex just a bit and that is enough to have be a little worried how it will hold up. Overall I do think its a good cup and the color is fantastic and bright so that makes me super happy!

title: Great little product! Hard-boiled eggs are no longer a challenge!
body: Bought this about two years ago because the egg-timer I had that actually sits in the boiling water with the eggs wasn't working as consistently as I had hoped. I saw this Dash Rapid egg Cooker and decided to give it a try. Love it! Mostly the eggs are cooked to satisfactory doneness, although occasionally a smidge under (but not grossly) or a tad over, but definitely cooked and edible. I found that using the 4+ level on the measuring cup for 6 eggs and filling it to just above that mark is enough water to cook the eggs to the required doneness. I've used the omelet feature a couple of times and it's okay, but mainly I use the DASH Rapid Egg Cooker for making hard-boiled eggs. My husband loves hard-boiled eggs so this was a great way to make sure he had his weekly quota. I would recommend this product! I've read other reviews where the purchaser said there's no way to store the trays and other paraphernalia, which is not exactly true. The trays (there are three; one for boiled eggs, one for scrambled, and a split tray for maybe omelets) fit conveniently inside the unit, with the stem and measuring/poking cup nestled in the split tray. I've not had any issues with them not fitting. And there is just one way to use the stem (I rarely use it, though) and it fits just one way, too. date: Reviewed in the United States on February 9, 2026
title: Simple, inexpensive Egg Cooker makes perfect soft-boiled/hardboiled eggs!!!!!
body: I love poached eggs but using 2 or 3 cups of water for 2 eggs and waiting for the water to boil was annoying, not to mention having to clean a teflon-free pan with egg glomming onto the sides.... until I found this little thing. I don't actually use the little plastic poaching pan that comes with this, I just stand them up on the grid and steam them. Then I do like the Brits do, whack off the top and either eat it right out of shell or spoon it on to a plate. Perfect every time! Uses a couple of tablespoons of water compared to a whole pan and cooks in about 5 minutes cutting my consumption of electricity and water! I And no cleaning egg residue out of the saucepan! I concede I'm not as handy as the Brits whacking off the top but bought an egg cracker that does the job quite well. And it would be a good idea to have at least one egg cup to hold the egg while you open it because it's VERY hot right out of the steamer and could be more than a little messy without the egg cup! It also does a great job hard boiling eggs too, also saving time and water. I've used this little gizmo 4 or 5 times a week for 2 1/2 years and it's still steaming away. The tray that heats the water needs to be wiped down pretty often as the steam/water removes the "bloom" from the eggshell and, combined with the chemicals in the water, can get a little crusty. However, that crusty stuff stays on the bottom and doesn't touch the egg inside the shell and comes off easily with a little soapy soak. I don't use a micro-wave and won't cook food in plastic, but since these eggs stay in the shell and nothing plastic touches the inner-egg it's about as pristine a process as I can make it, conserves energy, cuts the wait time considerably and makes perfect soft-boiled and hard-boiled eggs! It's a genius little cooker! (I gave 7 to friends and family for Christmas! ) So simple and inexpensive! Read more Read more

title: Powerful Upgrade: My Experience with the Ninja Blender
body: After years of using my Magic Bullet, I decided it was time for an upgrade and chose a Ninja blender because I’ve always known Ninja to be a higher-quality brand. One of the main reasons I switched was that I wanted a larger cup size so I could make bigger smoothies without having to split them into multiple batches. The price of this blender also felt very fair for the quality and brand reputation, which made the decision even easier. One thing I really appreciate about this blender is how straightforward it is to use. There is essentially no assembly required and no complicated buttons to figure out. You simply screw the blade topper onto the cup, place it onto the machine, and press down to start blending. The only downside to this design is that you can’t operate it hands-free—you do have to hold it in place while it blends. That said, it works incredibly fast. It didn’t even take a full minute to completely blend frozen fruit, ice, and the other ingredients in my smoothie. Another feature I liked is that this set came with three cups and multiple lids, including tops that make it easy to drink your smoothie on the go. Having the extra cups is convenient if you’re making drinks for more than one person or prepping ahead. Overall, I’m really happy with this purchase and feel like it was a worthwhile upgrade from my Magic Bullet. Read more Read more

product_name: Hamilton Beach Power Elite Wave Action Blender For Smoothies, 40 Oz Glass Jar, 12 Functions Including Puree, Crush Ice, 700 Watts - Black (58148A)
date: Reviewed in the United States on September 15, 2014
title: Great blender, so far - and a recipe for a delicious smoothie!
body: I picked this up after being unable to find a blender under $30 in any local stores that had decent reviews (I always check Amazon.com for pricing and reviews before purchasing anything). I had purchased a VERY similar model (same brand, and similar features) on impulse at a local store for right at $30, but decided not to even take it out of the box after reading the reviews on Amazon (my phone was dead when I purchased it, or I would have checked the reviews before pulling the trigger). After a bit of research I came across this unit which was a few dollars less, and is the same brand with almost identical features - so I was surprised that it had so much better reviews, but I figured it was worth the gamble and I needed a new blender for my morning smoothies ASAP (my old one was not working at-all). I never took the other one out of the box, so I can't compare this one to that one - but I can say that I'm very happy with this blender - it works perfectly for what I need it for, and although I have only had it for a few weeks, I haven't had any problems yet and it seems sturdy enough to last for a while. The problem with my last blender was that the gears the connected the motor in the base to the blades in the pitcher were made of rubber or soft plastic, so over the years they eventually got stripped until they were unable to catch each other enough to make the blades spin. During my search, I came across several models that had metal pieces connecting the blades to the motor which seems like it would be ideal. This model uses plastic, however it seems thick and sturdy enough to last a while. The pitcher is also very wide all the way to the bottom. Many blenders have a 'funnel' shaped pitcher that is smaller at the bottom (by the blades), sometimes causing ice or frozen chunks to get caught in the blades which can cause problems with the motor and/or the gears that connect the blades to the motor. Because the bottom of this is so wide, it seems like this happens less frequently because the blades will simply push the frozen chunks aside rather than getting them stuck between the blades and the glass. I rarely used the lid on my old blender, as I don't usually have issues with the ingredients splashing out - however, the lid of this blender has a couple of features that I really like. One is the spout that allows you to more easily pour your smoothies into your cups with less mess/dripping, and the other is a little 'flap' that allows you to easily pour in ingredients while blending without worrying about splashing. date: Reviewed in the United States on July 28, 2023
title: Wish I had returned this
body: Updating from Oct 2024 (purchased July 2023): This blender really isn’t good, or at least maybe I got a dud. Currently my blender only works 1/5 times and I have to finagle it around to get it to start. A weird smoky burning smell happens when it’s running as well. It also leaves odd black marks all over my (white!) countertops, and I can’t figure out if this is the rubber on the bottom or if literally something is coming out of it. It’s basically just a paper weight at this point. And all I use it for is making smoothies. It rarely even blends up all the fruit. Not a great use of my money even if it was a ‘good price.’ It’s a loss if you have to replace it every year. Ugh. Original: I don't know about this blender. I bought it during Prime Day because my old blender (a Black & Decker one I got in college and probably got on a Black Friday deal) had recently broken. There are so many blender options available that it is quite overwhelming to pick one especially with all the features they have, but since my old B&D was "basic," I figured I didn't need like a Ninja-level blender for my purposes (I literally plan to only make things like smoothies or milkshakes with this). The only requirement I wanted was a blender with a glass jar. this is just what I am used to , but I also noticed that many blenders with plastic jars (including a ninja one I was considering) had reviews that stated plastic jars may crack. So, with that being part of my search criteria, I saw this Hamilton Beach one for an ok price and it had many positive reviews, so I decided to pick it up. With that said, when I received this blender I already missed my B&D one in "look and feel." While I think the glass jar is a nice size/shape, I don't like the quality of the lid. I thought having a pour spout would be nice, but it just feels so flimsy and cheap and when you use it it's like another messy thing to clean. I'm not sure what it adds on just pouring straight from the glass pitcher. Plus I find it cheap and plastic-y and it is difficult to pull off of the glass jar (I've literally damaged my nails trying to release it). In terms of function, I don't think this is all that powerful. I have probably used it about 5-7 times so far, and I'd say 40% of the time I've had to "assist" the blender to process my food because it will get stuck. I have literally only made smoothies with this (and that is all I really intend to use it for). I followed the directions, saying put in liquids first, followed by fruit. My blender literally only has milk, yogurt, and frozen fruit, and it is not able to effectively blend that consistently without me having to open it and push the fruit down with a spoon. The "wave" action doesn't seem to do much. It is also very loud. It think it is louder when it is probably working harder (like when it is blending and not pulling the fruit down). But it really sounds like a vacuum cleaner. So that is annoying because I didn't have to stop and assist the blender on my old B&D. Since I don't think a smoothie is all that complicated/difficult to blend, the fact that it can't do that consistently isn't very encouraging. So I am considering sending this back. If I can find a similarly priced blender to this, I will likely swap it for that. Read more Read more

date: Reviewed in Canada on September 26, 2016
title: Good warranty service (at least in Canada!), good blender for the price point
body: I've used this blender more or less on a daily basis with frozen fruits and vegetables for about two months. I knew that I would be getting a lot of use out of it, and have had experience with both low end blenders (always disappointing) and high end blenders (from working at a smoothie making business), and was a little bit hesitant to purchase one at this price point, rather than investing in a higher end, more powerful one. However, I was happily surprised by both the quality and easy of use of this blender. It has a very solid glass jar (I've had bad experiences with cracks in the jars of cheaper plastic blenders). It also comes apart for easy cleaning and can go in the dishwasher. It's so easy to clean and used so frequently in my household that I rarely need to put it in the dishwasher unless I'm in a rush. The motor may not be as powerful as some more high end blenders, but as long as you make sure to start with a liquid base, it has no trouble blending frozen foods. The only caveat that I have is that, about a month into use, my unit started leaking. At first, I thought it was because I was putting it back together incorrectly, but then I noticed a small crack in the plastic/rubber ring in the cutter assembly. After wondering how difficult it would be to get the part replaced, I continued using it for a few weeks, but finally made the call to the Hamilton Beach office in Canada today. Read more Read more

title: Easy clean-up, no leaking, works well, good price
body: Using these with my Cosori 5.8qt air fryer; the liner fits well. When inserting the liner into the preheated air fryer basket, be careful not to burn your hand. I use a spoon or other object to help place the liner flat in the preheated basket. I have used these several times over the past week and never had oil or grease leak through. Even when cooking hamburger meat, which is very greasy! Feels great to just take out the dirty liner and throw it away, leaving a clean air fryer basket for the next meal. Easy! Follow the directions and the liner should work well. Do not place the empty liner in the air fryer while preheating. Preheat the air fryer with nothing in the basket. When it’s ready to cook, put the liner in the basket and be sure to weigh down the liner with food. If there is not enough food on the liner the liner could fly into the heating element and catch on fire. So be sure to weigh down the liner with food first. Then you could start air frying. So far I have not had any burns/fires with these liners. Very pleased with this product. Because I don’t need to wash the air fryer basket after meals, I am using the air fryer more often! Great product.

title: Great and the weight is a good thing see below
body: After I learned to season it quick like 7 times, and started using a bush and cup with a dap of oil on the side I kept around to wipe with oil when done, and learned to due to size I needed to have rag on edge of sink when dumping out water, and then I often but not always heat up water in electric kettle to pour in when done cooking in order to not shock it. I find after all this that I actually do basically no scrubbing, just a bit of scraping with the metal spatula while the hot water is in it. And then I use a silicone handle on one side I had gotten and I want to get the other side for the silicone side to hold but I just use a pot holder for the other side, thicker one. But I find now that this is an ideal pan to cook everything and anything and many things. I just leave it on top of my stove for daily use. And the weight, for the same reason a mechanics arms are big while not lifting, their muscles are big purely due to making the same motion every day repetitively with the wrench. It’s called a hermetic stressor, the same way weight session (more stressful obviously) and a hot sauna, cold plunge, run, etc, are all good forms of stress that cause adaptation, So to does this just cause you to lift a heavier pot a few times a day as you cook. For vast majority of us it’s nothing. But I told my mom for instance that she should use it and she complained how heavy it was, and my brutal honest response was “that’s exactly why you need to be using it because it’s not like you are working out”. It won’t make you buff, just a bit heavier than a similiar size pan, but for the older crowd who find it important to get their exercises in at the pool and such, this is no different. Plus, once I learned to clean efficiently and season a couple times, it’s a god send of a pan. I love it. I just want to put that “it’s too heavy” criticisism that comes with cast iron in a new light. Your body adapts, allow it too gradually. I will at some point be adding a top to this, I just haven’t decided if I need to get the lodge glass one which would be nice or a silicone one for this, or just a cast iron one to keep the theme and look and durability forever. (Glass and silicone could both break in different ways). I do love this pan as I will admit, one of the reason I got this pan, being some one who can be hard on things by temperment, after knowing roughly how to care for this pan, I also have a lisence to absolutely abuse it and can’t scratch it or anything. Because once you have researched a couple of ways to take rust off and to totally reseason and recondition an old used on for instance, you have the confidence to own this the rest of your life and not ever feel like your going to rune it. I’m also strategically lazy, I call efficient, my mom thinks different, lol, but anyway, the fastest way to clean any pan is with hot water right when done cooking as it burns and melts stuff right off, I can do that with this without worrying how it affects coatings or anything and I can do that to kingdom come. I just try to throw water from kettle on it ideally but not always. Again, the point of these pans is the amazing non stick coating that develops after use and learning to season a few more times your self, but that you can absolutely abuse these pans and know they will last. There is a certain security that’s nice to feel with knowing that. The fact that it has a great non stick that develops after some use and is durable Af, I’m in love. Read more Read more

product_name: Lodge Seasoned Cast Iron Skillet - PFAS-Free, Non-Toxic Cookware - Use with an Oven, Stove, Grill, or Campfire - Naturally Non-Stick & Oven Safe - 10.25 Inches
date: Reviewed in the United States on November 17, 2007
title: Timeless classic for the modern kitchen
body: Sorry for the long review - for the short review, count the stars! I'm a bit of a purist. I always season my cast iron - new, or used (hey, I don't know WHAT someone else used that old piece of cast iron for - maybe cleaning auto parts). I sand it down to bare metal, starting with about an 80 grit and finishing with 200. Then I season. The end result is a glossy black mirror that puts Teflon to shame. There are two mistakes people make when seasoning - not hot enough, not long enough. These mistakes give the same result - a sticky brown coating that is definitely not non-stick, and the first time they bring any real heat to the pan, clouds of smoke that they neither expected or wanted. I see several complaints here that are completely due to not knowing this. But there were a few pieces I needed (yes, needed, cast iron isn't about want, it's a need), and this was one of them, so I thought I'd give the Lodge pre-seasoning a try. Ordered last Friday, received this Friday - free shipping, yay! The first thing I noticed was the bumpy coating. The inside is actually rougher than the outside, and my hand was itching for the sandpaper, but that would have defeated the experiment. This time, I was going to give the Lodge pre-seasoning a chance before I broke out the sandpaper. So I scrubbed the pan out with a plastic brush and a little soapy water, rinsed well, put it on a medium burner, and waited. Cast iron tip number one - give it a little time. Then give it a little more time. Cast iron conducts heat much more slowly than aluminum, so you have to have a little patience. Read more Read more

title: I cook almost everything in my Lodge Cast Iron Pan
body: If you haven't already taken the leap into cast iron you should think about it. First off the pan is cheap, durable and generally non-stick. Does this mean nothing will stick to this pan? No but if you have the right temperature and use the right amount of fat/oil when cooking and take care of this pan it will take care of you. I would say this pan excels at proteins first and foremost. I have cooked bacon, eggs and pancakes right after one another and nothing stuck to this pan. I would say vegetables are also a favorite in this pan as well because of the great flavor this pan helps create through the oil and char you can achieve with this pan. If you are the type of person that cooks and then puts the pans in the sink for a later time then cast iron might not be for you but I have left this pan in a sink for a few hours or on the stove top overnight and cleaned the next day so it is worth a shot. If you are the type of person who lets pans soak in water overnight or puts them immediately into a dishwasher for cleaning then this pan is definitely not for you unless you are willing to make an exception with this pan. Did I have immediate success with this pan right away? No I didn't. I had food stick and I had issues with cleaning the pan and reseasoning the pan and had to look for advice on the internet to solve my issues because not many people use cast iron so I couldn't ask them.
