date: Reviewed in the United States on March 30, 2026
title: Turn the unit off after your food is "Ready" or use "warm" setting
body: This air fryer is everything they say it is and I highly recommend it The ceramic non stick coating really sealed the deal for me However, I notice that when food is finished cooking or displays"Ready" if I don't turn the unit off after about 5 minutes I smell plastic The unit was set up and prepared for use as per instructions and I've already cooked with it a few times with no problems but I would always pull the basket out and push it back in, or turn the unit off after cooking I suspect the AC coil may still be activated without the fan running Without the DC fan running it could potentially heat up some plastic and if that AC coil does indeed remain active then I would suggest to turn the unit off or unplug the unit I will update if this happens again but I do know that I smelled plastic This could be a design flaw but only time will tell I would suggest to turn the unit off or unplug it when your food is "Ready" or use the "Warm" setting if you must let it sit This could be a big deal if this is indeed a design flaw Other than that, this is probably the best air fryer for the money Just remember to pull the drawer out and push it back in to in to reset the unit to idle Don't let it just sit there with the "Ready" light remaining on Just takes a second or less to tap on the screen to shut it off or pull the tray out and push it back in You could also use the "Warm" feature which does use the DC powered fan with the low heat I hope I am wrong about this because I really love this air fryer and the ceramic non stick coating that it has, and It cooks very very well Again, there really is no need to have the "Ready" light on with the coil still activated It should have an after cooking timer like the instant pot that tells you how long it's been sitting there for reference But the instant pot also has the keep warm feature turned on by default, so you would also have to turn that off or some amount of heat would still be used, but it is designed that way and explained in the users manual This Cosori unit is supposed to shut itself off after 30 minutes of inactivity, but not trusting in that if I smell any plastic fumes I am still keeping the unit despite this potential design flaw I can live with that when everything else about this unit is perfectly fine Read more Read more

title: Outstanding Device, Cosori!
body: Because we ran somewhat late after working in the yard, we decided to cook frozen Fish Sticks and French Fries in this Air Fryer for our first use of it. Our first reaction was, WOW! This air fryer took these simple foods to another level! Our fish sticks & fries turned out crisp and flavorful; better than we've ever had them before. In fact, we didn't even realize these quick, easy foods could be that good. Prior to getting this Air Fryer, we had always prepared our fish & fries in our big oven at 420 degrees. And, they always took over 20 minutes each; so the fries would be cooled and somewhat wrinkled and shrunken, while trying to keep them warm in the small microwave warmer as we cooked the fish sticks. With this Air Fryer, the fries were done in about 15 minutes and the fish sticks about 12 min; a little over 25 mines combined. We were astonished at how much oil drained out of the sticks & fries through the crisper plate to the bottom of the fryer basket. We didn't realize that these frozen foods had so much oil in them, and how much they retained, when lying in their own oil on a flat cookie sheet to heat. We just wish you had the same model and features with a 7 or 8 qt. size basket that would easily allow cooking a whole 6-7lb chicken or turkey. Lastly, we very much appreciated how effortless the non-stick ceramic crisper plate fryer drawer cleaned up... simple! Outstanding food prep device Cosori! Something to be proud of!

title: Schnell, leise, einfach zu bedienen
body: Die Turbo Blaze ist schön schnell, leiser als die Ninja Af180 (um die 2-4 db) und ist leicht zu bedienen. Es ist sehr einfach, tolle Ergebnisse zu produzieren. Ein Schnitzel, oder Pommes vom Vortag: in Minuten wieder knusprig! HotdogWürstchen: in Minuten knusprig. Ergebnis besser als im Ofen- hier kommen meine Sachen zwar fertig, aber auch irgendwie ledrig raus. Hat sie einen Mehrwert in Vergleich zum Ofen? Definitiv! Durch Geschwindigkeit und Einfachheit. Wer wirft schon 1 Würstchen oder eine Scheibe Brot in den Ofen oder auf die Pfanne? Mit der Cosori kann man das machen, da es eine Minutensache ist. Da sie wenig Platz braucht, ist sie auch ideal für den Balkon. Mal eben ohne Dreck und Rauch was brutzeln-toll. Als Resteveredler sehr genial. Das Display reagiert schnell und kann man von verschiedenen Winkeln sehr gut ablesen. Die Abluft schießt nicht wie beim Ninja einfach gerade nach hinten, sondern wird nach oben und zur Seite verwirbelt. Gut, falls man hinten nicht viel Platz oder eine empfindliche Wand hat. Ich hatte allerdings auch starke Geruchsprobleme bei meinem 1. Gerät. Selbst nach fast 8 Stunden "ausbrennen" roch es immer noch zu stark nach Plastik in der Wohnung. Hier scheint Cosori in der Produktion wohl ein Problem zu haben, wenn man die vielen Meinungen zum langanhaltenden Geruch liest. Eine Anfrage per Mail am Freitagmittag an Cosori wurde fast umgehend beantwortet und es wurde sich sehr schnell und sehr freundlich um ein Austauschgerät gekümmert. Wollte ich nach der Erfahrung mit dem Geruch eine andere Marke? Irgendwie nicht, denn die Turbo hatte zuvor einfach zuviel Spaß gemacht! So.simple. Das 2. Gerät hat anfangs ebenfalls gestunken (wie die Ninja übrigens auch), aber nach 1 Stunde war der Plastikgeruch zum Glück weg. Ich habe gute Erfahrung mit dieser Lösung gemacht: Read more Read more

title: Amazing, but gigantic. Gave to my partner.
body: 5 stars because I got this bottle on sale for the same price as the 30oz, the colors are *gorgeous* and every bit as vibrant as the image shows (which I'm seeing is not typical across the board for owala) and it is indeed just a fabulous, strong water bottle. Easy to drink from the built-in straw, a joy to carry, and fits in every cup holder so far. I've already adapted better hydrating habits compared to using my old daily Nalgene. However, I am 5'5 and wanted a bottle to carry around frequently for work. This thing is like as big as my thigh bone lol! I should have gone to a local Target or Academy to compare the 30oz vs the 40oz bottle because the size difference is significant. If you consider the added weight for a full 40oz, I set myself up for failure in terms of my needs for this bottle. My 6'2" boyfriend who loves colors and works outside all day was happy to adopt this one for me however, and I ended up purchasing a 30oz elsewhere in equally fun colors. We have both had success having ice last a full workday. His keeps cold in the car and mine usually has a good bit of ice still after 3 refills and constant use. So far so good on no-spills, and I really appreciate the lock mechanism as I work around delicate artwork all day. It would be nice if the cups were machine washable alongside the gaskets, lid, and straw-- but alas, it's easy enough to clean in the sink and probably wouldn't fit in a top rack anyway. Each bottle comes wrapped with beautifully thorough care and use instructions as well-- take the time to read it all! Read more Read more

title: Great Water Bottle!
body: I was looking for an insulated water bottle that would actually fit in a standard cup holder. I decided to try the Owala FreeSip Sway Insulated Stainless Steel Water Bottle. Upon receiving it, I noticed that it felt solid in my hand. The mechanism worked very smoothly with a simple button press opening the lid. Once open, you can choose to either use the included straw or tip the bottle and drink from the chug spout. In addition to it fitting in my cup holder, I needed it to be leak proof. This bottle is leak proof, and the spout can be locked by engaging a lock in the back. The rugged flip handle is also a convenient way to carry the Owala when it's not in the water bottle holder on my backpack. I hand wash the body of the bottle, but the top disassembles easily and can go right in the dishwasher when I want a deep cleaning. The bottle has a wide opening, so it is very easy to clean the inside. This has quickly become my favorite water bottle due to its ease of cleaning, good insulation, ability to fit in my cup holder, solid construction and materials, and the option of using the straw or chug spout. I have the 30 ounce bottle, but I am going to be picking up a 40 ouncer also. You won't regret purchasing one! I have used the bottle for a couple of weeks now, and here are my observations: It has gone on two camping trips, and it has proven to be durable. I have placed the Owala on its side on my passenger seat (just to test it...it fits perfectly in my car's cup holder!). It did not leak a single drop on a long road trip. On my camping trip, the water in the bottle was still ice cold the next morning, so it keeps cold beverages very cold! Read more Read more

body: Overall, these spandex tablecloths did a great job for our event setup. The material quality feels smooth and stretches tightly over the table for a very sleek, professional appearance. They’re wrinkle-resistant just as advertised, and even after folding and storing, they went right onto the tables looking clean and neat. We also noticed they handled small spills pretty well—stain resistance was decent, and cleanup was easy with just a damp cloth. The functionality is excellent: the elastic corners and snug fit mean they don’t shift around or flap in the wind, which makes them perfect for both indoor and outdoor use. The weight is light, which makes them super easy to transport and set up, but also means you may want to be a little careful if you're using them in high-traffic areas—they’re just a bit on the thin side. date: Reviewed in the United States on January 17, 2014
title: Amazing Pot, my favorite appliance ever.
body: I received my Instant Pot right before the end of the year as I had pre-ordered it. I had done a good amount of research on the previous model and was convinced it could replace my small slow cooker as well as diversify my cooking and reduce my time in the kitchen. The main improvements I liked on this model over the previous model was the holder for the lid on both sides of the handles of the pot, the larger display, and the main improvement I was interested in was the dual pressure setting (mainly comes in handy for veggies for me). At first use it took a little getting used to this new appliance. Some of the features which take some getting used to for me were the pressure release valve, and how to use the electronic controls. When cooking in a pressure cooker using traditional methods it is necessary to set the pressure release valve to closed, this allows pressure to build up in the pot during cooking. During the first few times I had forgotten to twist the pressure release valve to closed. Not doing this will eventually cause a light whistling sound and steam to come out of the release. Twist it back and all is well. I mention this because I read a review giving this pot a 1 star rating, and I could tell they hadn't closed their pressure valve based on the results they said they achieved. Read more Read more

title: From Pressure Cooking Novice to Culinary Master
body: As a self-proclaimed cooking enthusiast, I recently embarked on a culinary adventure with the Instant Pot Duo 7-in-1 Electric Pressure Cooker, and boy, what a game-changer it has been! This marvel of a kitchen appliance has not only simplified my cooking process but has also elevated my meals to a whole new level. Let me share my delightful experience with you. First off, the title of "7-in-1" is no joke! This versatile appliance seamlessly transforms into a pressure cooker, slow cooker, rice cooker, steamer, sauté pan, yogurt maker, warmer, and even a sterilizer. It's like having an entire kitchen arsenal in one compact device. I couldn't help but admire the sleek stainless steel design that added a touch of elegance to my countertop. Getting started was a breeze, thanks to the user-friendly controls and the accompanying free app, boasting a whopping 1900 recipes. The app became my culinary companion, guiding me through various cuisines and offering endless inspiration. I was particularly thrilled by the app's meal planning feature, which made my life much easier. Now, let's talk about the real magic—the pressure cooking feature. It dramatically reduced cooking times, allowing me to prepare mouthwatering stews, roasts, and soups in a fraction of the time. I recall the first time I attempted a beef stew, and within 30 minutes, the meat was tender, succulent, and bursting with flavors that would typically take hours to develop. Read more Read more

product_name: Instant Pot Duo 7-in-1 Electric Pressure Cooker, Slow Cooker, Rice, Steamer, Sauté, Yogurt Maker, Warmer & Sterilizer, Includes App With Over 800 Recipes, Stainless Steel, 6 Quart
date: Reviewed in the United States on April 16, 2016
title: I LOVE My Instant Pot! But Here's What I Wish I'd Known when I First Got It
body: I LOVE my Instant Pot! But I will be the first to admit that it can be a little intimidating at first, and it can feel like it has a steep learning curve (and I'm a tech reviewer and tech lawyer, and one of those people who generally just jumps in and figures things out without reading the manuals ("Manuals? We don't need no stinkin' manuals!"), so for me to feel like I'm not 'getting' something ..well, yeah. So if you are feeling a little bewildered by your new Instant Pot, *you're not alone*!) Plus, the manual does *not* include certain information that I, at least, was looking for. Such as, how long is each pre-programmed cooking cycle? Exactly what temperature do the various sauté settings heat to? Things like that. So, here are a few tips that have really helped me to finally 'get' it, plus instructions for two things that you can make in your Instant Pot that will change your life: incredibly easy perfectly poached eggs in 2-3 minutes, and baked potatoes in 12 minutes. First, it is almost impossible to mess up with this thing to a point of being dangerous, so if you're concerned about the exploding pressure cookers of yore, you needn't be (I said "almost", don't go overriding your pot's safety features and then blame me when you poke an eye out). The lid audibly tells you when its sealed (when you turn it clockwise), and the pot won't even build up much pressure if you haven't properly closed the steam release handle by turning it, too, clockwise. The most likely point at which a problem could arise would be if you try to open the lid (by turning it counter-clockwise) before all of the pressure has been released and normalized (so don't do that). The pot visually lets you know when it's safe to open the pot, by the float valve (the little silver post that pops up when the pot is pressurized) dropping back down flush with the lid instead of being popped up. Think of the float valve as the reverse of a turkey pop-up button, in the case of the float valve it's done when the button pops *in*, instead of out. Read more Read more

body: I purchased the Instant Pot Duo 6QT in January, and since then it has been extremely useful in my daily cooking, especially as I mostly prepare South Indian meals — it is very very convenient and saves a lot of time, with super fast rice cooking that comes out perfectly every time, soft and fluffy without any hassle; from morning breakfast to dinner it handles everything smoothly, idli steaming turns out soft and spongy, dosa batter fermentation is consistent, sambar develops a rich, well-cooked flavour in much less time, rasam stays perfectly balanced without over boiling, and even dishes like chicken curry or mutton kulambu come out tender with spices deeply infused as if slow-cooked for hours; the sauté function is excellent for tempering mustard seeds, curry leaves, and hing just like traditional cooking, and it works perfectly for dal, vegetables, and even chickpeas with great texture and taste, while also preserving nutrition; cleaning is simple and the build quality feels solid and reliable, so overall it combines traditional South Indian taste with modern speed and convenience, making it a must-have appliance — VERY VERY GOOD and highly recommended for everyday cooking.

date: Reviewed in Canada on November 23, 2017
title: A truly Canadian innovation - Instant Pot is the best item in my kitchen, hands down
body: •• Why I Bought The Instant Pot (and why you probably should, too) •• In this review, I'll cover safety mechanisms and info, how to use the instant pot, minimum liquid requirements, along with a whole slew of things I learned over a year and a half of ownership. I waited a long time to write this because I wanted to be sure the Instant Pot wasn't a flash in the pan gimmick. How was the quality of construction? Did it maintain heating standards over the years? How often do parts need replacement? I had so many questions! The technology was too new for definitive answers. Yes, it has thousands of positive reviews, but so many people tend to review the first moment they get a product and never update. I wanted answer, so I took the plunge FOR SCIENCE! (Also delicious food, but mostly science) I love food, and I love cooking. While not amazing, I am a passionate home cook with a wide variety of culinary interests. I make meals for my wife daily, and I refuse to buy cheap, nutritionally absent lunches. As such, I'm almost always in the kitchen plugging away. The truth is... the Instant Pot is brilliant, and the high praise it has received from reviewers is SPOT ON. It has made everything in my culinary life easier and better. I've used it constantly over a year and a half (originally purchased August, 2016), and it looks like it's fresh off the assembly line. I haven't even had to replace the least durable aspect (the silicon seal) due to my lack of acidic recipes. The stainless steel pot looks like it hasn't been subject to any heat, let alone hundreds of pressure-filled uses. Even if your pot did warp, you can buy a replacement through Amazon. Some people I know purchased a second simply so that they could start another meal without having to wash the first. Read more Read more
